subject: Economics
text: The ultimate aim of agricultural policies in Nigeria is to achieve
explanation: Nigeria's agricultural policies are primarily designed to ensure the country can feed itself, making food (self-)sufficiency the central goal.

subject: Economics
text: Government can boost agricultural output in Nigeria primarily by
explanation: Subsidizing farm inputs like fertilizer, seeds, and equipment lowers farmers' production costs, encouraging them to expand output.

subject: Economics
text: In developing countries, governments influence the location of industries in order to

options.D: encourage industries to earn high profits

explanation: Governments often direct industries to less-developed regions to spread economic growth, infrastructure, and job opportunities more evenly across the country.

subject: Economics
text: The primary motive for an individual engaging in production is to
explanation: In a market economy, individuals and firms are chiefly motivated to produce goods and services by the desire to earn profit.

subject: Economics
text: A country's population grew from 40,000 to 50,000 people, and its working population grew from 32,000 to 49,000 over the same period. What is the percentage of the working population out of the total population in the year when the totals were 40,000 and 32,000 respectively?
explanation: Working population as a percentage of total population is (32,000/40,000) times 100, which equals 80%, showing the share of people of working age.

subject: Economics
text: One of the characteristics of a free trade zone is

options.B: different trade policies of non-member countries
options.C: free factor mobility within the zone

explanation: In a free trade area, unlike a customs union, member countries remove tariffs among themselves but each retains its own independent tariff policy toward non-member countries.

subject: Economics
text: If Nigeria imports vehicles from Japan, the transaction will appear as a

options.A: debit on Japan's balance of payments
options.B: credit on Japan's balance of payments
options.C: credit on Nigeria's balance of trade
options.D: credit on Nigeria's balance of payments

explanation: An export sale earns foreign exchange for the exporting country, so Japan's sale of vehicles to Nigeria is recorded as a credit item on Japan's balance of payments.

subject: Economics
text: One of the objectives of the African Development Bank (ADB) is to

options.A: provide subsidies on imported goods to member countries
options.B: mobilize short-term loans for member countries
options.C: promote economic and social development of member countries
options.D: provide technical assistance to only poor member countries

explanation: The African Development Bank's core mandate is to finance projects and programmes that promote the economic and social development of its member countries.

subject: Economics
text: The choice of the method of production in an economy is determined by the
explanation: The technique a producer uses - labour-intensive or capital-intensive - largely depends on the level of technology and technical know-how available in the economy.

subject: Economics
text: An effective way of controlling inflation in a mixed economy is to
explanation: Boosting productivity increases the supply of goods and services without expanding the money supply, easing inflationary pressure while sustaining growth.

subject: Economics
text: A major disadvantage of the arithmetic mean is that it is

options.B: not suitable for further statistical analysis
options.C: cumbersome to determine the actual value

explanation: Because the mean uses every value in a data set, one very large or small (extreme) figure can pull the average away from what is truly typical.

subject: Economics
text: If the demand for one commodity excludes another, it is said to be
explanation: Goods that satisfy the same want and compete for the buyer's preference, such as tea versus coffee, exhibit competitive demand - choosing one excludes buying the other.

subject: Economics
text: The median of an odd-numbered set of scores is the
explanation: For an odd number of ordered observations, the median is simply the value that sits exactly in the middle of the arranged data.

subject: Economics
text: If demand increases without a change in supply, equilibrium price and quantity will
explanation: An increase in demand, with supply unchanged, shifts the demand curve rightward, moving the equilibrium point up the supply curve to a higher price and higher quantity.

subject: Economics
text: In the process of production, total output is at maximum when
explanation: Total product stops rising and reaches its peak exactly at the point where marginal product falls to zero - adding another unit of input no longer adds to total output.

subject: Economics
text: Given that price of N1 corresponds to a quantity supplied of 60 bags and price of N2 corresponds to a quantity supplied of 120 bags, the price elasticity of supply is
explanation: Percentage change in quantity is (120-60)/60, which is 100%, and percentage change in price is (2-1)/1, also 100%. Dividing gives an elasticity of exactly 1, meaning unitary elastic supply.

subject: Economics
text: When a consumer is at equilibrium, the MRSxy is equal to the
explanation: Consumer equilibrium occurs where the marginal rate of substitution between two goods equals the ratio of their prices, reflecting the trade-off the market offers.

subject: Economics
text: Minimum price legislation by government will
explanation: A minimum (floor) price set above equilibrium encourages producers to supply more while discouraging consumers, resulting in a persistent surplus of unsold goods.

subject: Economics
text: Ranking is the method used in measuring
explanation: The ordinal approach to utility does not measure satisfaction in numbers but instead ranks bundles of goods in order of preference - first, second, third, and so on.

subject: Economics
text: If a firm is faced with an elastic supply curve, its revenue will

options.A: be supplied at a higher price
options.C: increase by more than the percentage increase in price

explanation: With elastic supply, a given percentage rise in price causes a proportionally larger rise in quantity supplied, so total revenue increases by more than the price rise alone.

subject: Economics
text: A firm will experience diseconomies of scale when

options.A: there are difficulties in coordinating production
options.B: there is shortage in labour supply
options.C: the size of the market is small
options.D: there is an increase in the price of raw materials

explanation: As a firm grows very large, management and communication become harder to coordinate, raising average costs - the essence of diseconomies of scale.

subject: Economics
text: The law of variable proportions is applicable only
explanation: The law of variable proportions (diminishing returns) assumes at least one factor is fixed, a condition that holds only in the short run since all factors are variable in the long run.

subject: Economics
text: If real income increases while nominal income remains the same, it can be inferred that
explanation: Real income equals nominal income adjusted for price changes. If nominal income is unchanged but real income rises, the price level must have fallen, increasing the purchasing power of the same money income.

subject: Economics
text: One of the characteristics of oligopoly is the availability of
explanation: Oligopoly is a market structure dominated by a small number of large sellers, each with enough market power to affect price through their output decisions.

subject: Economics
text: The profit of a monopolist can be eliminated where price equals
explanation: When price equals average cost, total revenue equals total cost, leaving the firm with only normal profit and no economic (supernormal) profit.

subject: Economics
text: Bank consolidation policy in Nigeria is a measure to increase
explanation: Bank consolidation (mergers) was primarily designed to strengthen banks financially by raising their minimum capital requirements, making them more resilient and competitive.

subject: Economics
text: An increase in the circulation of money without a corresponding increase in output will lead to
explanation: When money supply grows faster than the output of goods and services, too much money chases too few goods, pushing the general price level up - inflation.

subject: Economics
text: In national income accounts, an item counted as part of government spending is
explanation: Government spending in national income accounting includes payments for goods and services actually purchased, such as wages paid to public workers, while pensions and welfare payments are transfer payments, not part of GDP.

subject: Economics
text: If aggregate demand is lower than total output in an economy, national income will
explanation: When aggregate demand falls short of total output, unsold goods pile up, prompting producers to cut back output and income, so national income falls toward a lower equilibrium.

subject: Economics
text: During the era of barter, money was generally in the form of
explanation: Before standardized coinage, ordinary valuable goods such as cattle, cowries, or grain served as commodity money, exchanged for their intrinsic usefulness.

subject: Economics
text: A country achieves economic development when there is

options.C: a sustained increase in per capita income
options.D: an even distribution of goods and services

explanation: Economic development is generally measured by a lasting rise in real income per person, reflecting genuine improvement in living standards over time.

subject: Economics
text: A country embarks on deficit financing in order to
explanation: Deficit financing means the government spends more than it earns, injecting extra funds into the economy to stimulate investment and economic activity.

subject: Economics
text: The import-substitution strategy of industrialization is meant to encourage
explanation: Import substitution aims to replace previously imported goods with locally manufactured alternatives, thereby boosting domestic production and saving foreign exchange.

subject: Economics
text: Upstream oil activities involve the
explanation: Upstream refers to the exploration and production stage of the oil industry, finding and extracting crude oil, before it moves downstream to refining and marketing.

subject: Economics
text: Agricultural production in Nigeria is constrained by
explanation: Many well-intentioned agricultural policies in Nigeria have failed to yield results mainly because of poor implementation on the ground, not lack of planning.

subject: Economics
text: The main function of NNPC is to

options.A: oversee the development of the oil sector

explanation: The Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation was established to manage and oversee the country's oil and gas sector on behalf of the government.

subject: Economics
text: Investing heavily in the agricultural sector will lead to
explanation: Heavy investment in agriculture directly raises farm output and productivity, the most immediate benefit being enough food to meet domestic needs.

subject: Economics
text: Long-term funds for investment projects are sourced from the
explanation: The capital market deals in long-term financial instruments like shares and bonds, making it the main source of long-term funding for investment projects, unlike the money market which handles short-term funds.

subject: Economics
text: A policy aimed at enhancing globalization of the Nigerian economy is
explanation: Deregulation removes government restrictions and opens up sectors to foreign competition and investment, integrating the domestic economy more closely with the global economy.

subject: Economics
text: The Malthusian theory of population is ineffective in modern economies because of
explanation: Malthus assumed food production grows only arithmetically, but modern technical progress in seeds, fertilizers, and mechanization has allowed food output to grow much faster, disproving his pessimistic prediction.

subject: Economics
text: Optimum population enables an economy to attain the highest level of
explanation: Optimum population is defined as the population size that maximizes output per head, given the available resources and technology of a country.

subject: Economics
text: The money paid per hour for work done is
explanation: Wage rate specifically refers to payment calculated per unit of time worked, such as per hour, distinguishing it from salary, which is usually a fixed periodic amount.

subject: Economics
text: Trade takes place because of
explanation: Countries and individuals trade because they can produce certain goods at a lower opportunity cost than others, gaining more by specializing and exchanging - the principle of comparative advantage.

subject: Economics
text: The World Trade Organization is responsible for

options.A: stabilizing and harmonization of oil prices
options.B: ensuring equal participation of countries in trade
options.D: minimizing obstacles to international trade and investment

explanation: The WTO's central mission is to reduce trade barriers such as tariffs and quotas, facilitating freer flow of goods, services, and investment between nations.

subject: Economics
text: A major disadvantage of a socialist economy is that

options.D: there is high level of unemployment

explanation: Under socialism, the state, not consumers, largely decides what is produced, so individual consumers lose the power to influence production through their spending choices - a loss of consumer sovereignty.

subject: Economics
text: The sign of the slope of a graph in economic analysis is important because it

options.A: shows whether a good is normal or inferior
options.C: reveals the magnitude of the change between variables
options.D: helps to determine the unit of measurement of variables

explanation: A positive or negative slope tells us whether two economic variables move in the same or opposite directions, revealing the nature of their relationship.

subject: Economics
text: A rise in income will, ceteris paribus, bring about

options.A: a movement along the demand curve
options.B: a leftward shift of the demand curve
options.C: a rightward shift of the demand curve
options.D: no effect on the demand curve

explanation: For a normal good, an increase in consumer income raises the quantity demanded at every price, shifting the entire demand curve rightward.

subject: Economics
text: Given that at a price of ₦50 the quantity demanded is 500kg, and at a price of ₦70 the quantity demanded is 400kg, calculate the price elasticity of demand
explanation: Percentage change in quantity is (400-500)/500, which is -20%, and percentage change in price is (70-50)/50, which is 40%. Dividing gives an elasticity of 0.5, showing demand is inelastic.

subject: Economics
text: Comparison of interpersonal utility is impossible because
explanation: Since satisfaction from consumption is a personal, subjective feeling that varies from person to person, there is no objective yardstick to compare one person's utility with another's.

subject: Economics
text: The supply of cocoa is influenced by
explanation: As an agricultural crop, cocoa's yield and supply are strongly affected by weather and seasonal growing conditions.

subject: Economics
text: If quantity supplied is constant irrespective of price changes, the supply elasticity is
explanation: When quantity supplied does not respond at all to price changes, the percentage change in quantity is zero, giving an elasticity of zero - perfectly inelastic supply.

subject: Economics
text: The invisible hand promotes the interests of
explanation: Adam Smith's invisible hand describes how individuals pursuing their own self-interest in a free market unintentionally promote the overall good and efficiency of society.

subject: Economics
text: Fixing price above equilibrium will cause

options.A: demand and supply to remain constant

explanation: A price floor set above equilibrium encourages producers to supply more, since price is higher, while quantity demanded falls, creating a market surplus.

subject: Economics
text: An important function of the price system is to

options.A: ensure that producers' profits remain high
options.C: allocate resources to most productive uses
options.D: protect the economic interests of government

explanation: In a market economy, prices act as signals that direct scarce resources toward their most valued and efficient uses, guided by supply and demand.

subject: Economics
text: The equilibrium point of a firm is attained at the point where the isoquant is
explanation: A firm minimizes cost for a given output level, or maximizes output for a given cost, at the point where an isoquant is tangent to an isocost line.

subject: Economics
text: Patents and copyrights enable monopolists to

options.A: determine the quality of their products
options.B: determine the scale of their products
options.C: restrict information flow to new firms

explanation: Patents and copyrights are legal barriers that give the holder exclusive rights to a product or process, blocking competitors from entering that market.

subject: Economics
text: A discriminatory monopoly is characterized by

options.A: a common elasticity in different markets
options.C: a finite elasticity in all markets

explanation: Price discrimination is profitable because the monopolist charges different prices in markets with different price elasticities of demand, charging more where demand is less elastic.

subject: Economics
text: Net National Product is derived by deducting
explanation: NNP is calculated by subtracting depreciation, or the capital consumption allowance, from GNP, accounting for the wearing out of capital used in production.

subject: Economics
text: The investment expenditure of an economy changes by ₦2 million and MPC is 0.75. What is the change in income?
explanation: The multiplier is 1/(1-MPC) = 1/0.25 = 4. Multiplying by the ₦2 million change in investment gives a change in income of ₦8 million.

subject: Economics
text: With MPC of 0.75, the multiplier is
explanation: The investment multiplier is calculated as 1/(1-MPC) = 1/(1-0.75) = 1/0.25 = 4.

subject: Economics
text: If a basket of commodities cost ₦120 in the base year and ₦240 in the current year, calculate the price index
explanation: Price index equals (current year cost divided by base year cost) times 100, which is (240/120) times 100, giving 200.

subject: Economics
text: The minimum amount which banks are required to deposit with the central bank is determined by
explanation: The cash reserve ratio is the fraction of deposits that commercial banks must hold with the central bank, a key tool for controlling money supply.

subject: Economics
text: The major function of the money market is to

options.C: stabilize the value of the local currency

explanation: The money market deals in short-term financial instruments like treasury bills, providing businesses and government with short-term liquidity.

subject: Economics
text: An ad valorem tax is imposed on
explanation: An ad valorem tax is charged as a percentage of the value of a good, unlike a specific tax, which is a fixed amount per unit regardless of value.

subject: Economics
text: Rapid economic development in Nigeria is realizable by
explanation: Relying on a single commodity like oil exposes the economy to price shocks; diversifying into agriculture, manufacturing, and services builds a more resilient, sustainable economy.

subject: Economics
text: The primary reason for desiring economic growth is to
explanation: Economic growth expands the total goods and services available in an economy, which is the fundamental means of improving people's overall standard of living.

subject: Economics
text: A strategy for improving agriculture in Nigeria will involve

options.A: controlling the prices of agricultural products

explanation: A key agricultural improvement strategy targets food self-sufficiency, ensuring the country can meet its domestic food needs without excessive reliance on imports.

subject: Economics
text: A sugar industry is best located near the source of
explanation: Sugar cane is heavy and perishable, so processing plants are usually sited close to the farms, the raw material source, to reduce transport costs and spoilage.

subject: Economics
text: An important contribution of small-scale industries to the Nigerian economy is in the area of
explanation: Small-scale industries are typically labour-intensive, making their most significant contribution to the Nigerian economy the large number of jobs they create.

subject: Economics
text: A change in the pump price of petrol in Nigeria has a direct effect on the
explanation: Petrol is the primary fuel for road transportation in Nigeria, so any change in its pump price immediately raises or lowers transportation costs, which then ripple through to other prices.

subject: Economics
text: The Malthusian theory was concerned about the relationship between

options.A: population growth rates of the rich and the poor nations
options.C: population growth rate and natural resources

explanation: Malthus's theory centered on the tension between population growing geometrically and natural resources or food supply growing only arithmetically, leading to eventual scarcity.

subject: Economics
text: In a village of 50 persons, 10 immigrated, 25 died, and 5 emigrated in a year. Determine the total population
explanation: Total population equals the original population plus immigrants minus deaths minus emigrants: 50 + 10 - 25 - 5, which equals 30.

subject: Economics
text: A deficit balance of payments is measured by subtracting the debits from the credits in the
explanation: The overall balance of payments position is assessed by netting debits against credits across both the current account, covering trade, services, and income, and the capital account.

subject: Economics
text: One of the functions of ECOWAS is to

options.D: provide employment for citizens of member nations

explanation: ECOWAS works to support regional economic integration among West African states, including mobilizing funds for infrastructural projects that benefit member countries.

subject: Economics
text: An economy in which both the public and private sectors contribute to economic growth is known as
explanation: A mixed economy combines elements of both a market (private) system and government (public) intervention, with both sectors contributing to production and growth.

subject: Economics
text: If the standard deviation of a given data is 53, what is the variance?
explanation: Variance is the square of the standard deviation: 53 squared equals 2,809.

subject: Economics
text: An advantage of the range as a measure of dispersion is that it

options.A: can be used to calculate open-ended distribution
options.B: makes use of all values of observations in a distribution
options.D: is useful for further statistical calculation

explanation: Unlike the standard deviation, the range only needs the highest and lowest values, so it can still be found even when a distribution has open-ended classes.

subject: Economics
text: Find the median of the following set of data: 35, 10, 14, 38, 15, 18, 22, 30 and 28
explanation: Arranged in order the data is 10, 14, 15, 18, 22, 28, 30, 35, 38. With 9 values, the median is the 5th value, which is 22.

subject: Economics
text: An increase in demand without a corresponding change in supply will lead to

options.A: a decrease in equilibrium price and increase in equilibrium quantity
options.B: an increase in equilibrium price and quantity
options.C: a decrease in equilibrium price and quantity
options.D: an increase in equilibrium price and a decrease in equilibrium quantity

explanation: A rightward shift in demand, with supply unchanged, moves the equilibrium point up along the supply curve, raising both price and quantity.

subject: Economics
text: An increase in the price of a commodity will result in

options.A: a decrease in the quantity demanded

explanation: Per the law of demand, a rise in price causes a movement along the demand curve to a lower quantity demanded, holding other factors constant.

subject: Economics
text: If the price of a bicycle changes from ₦120 to ₦80 and quantity bought changes from 300 to 500 units, the elasticity of demand for the bicycle is
explanation: Percentage change in quantity is (500-300)/300, about 66.7%, and percentage change in price is (80-120)/120, about -33.3%. Elasticity, ignoring sign, is 66.7 divided by 33.3, about 2.0.

subject: Economics
text: One of the assumptions of the cardinalist approach is

options.B: the consistency and transitivity of choice
options.C: that total utility depends on the quantity of the commodities consumed

explanation: The cardinalist theory assumes utility can be measured in numerical units, and that the total satisfaction a consumer gets depends on how much of the good is consumed.

subject: Economics
text: Utility is the satisfaction derived from the
explanation: Utility refers to the want-satisfying power of a good, which a consumer only experiences by using or consuming it.

subject: Economics
text: If the price of a commodity is fixed below equilibrium, this will lead to
explanation: A price set below the market equilibrium causes quantity demanded to exceed quantity supplied, creating a shortage, or excess demand.

subject: Economics
text: One of the criticisms of the price mechanism is that

options.B: it provides low degree of freedom

explanation: A major criticism of relying purely on market prices is that it can worsen inequality, since those with more purchasing power get more resources regardless of need.

subject: Economics
text: In Nigeria, government can reduce the cost of accommodation by fixing rent
explanation: Setting a maximum rent below the market equilibrium price directly lowers what tenants must pay, though it can also cause housing shortages.

subject: Economics
text: If a refinery achieves a reduction in cost by purchasing and transporting crude oil in large quantities, it enjoys
explanation: Buying and transporting inputs in bulk lowers the average cost per unit, which is a form of economies of scale, specifically purchasing and marketing economies.

subject: Economics
text: An isoquant lying above and to the right of another represents
explanation: Isoquants further from the origin represent combinations of inputs that produce greater levels of output.

subject: Economics
text: A measure of national income used as comparison of standard of living among nations is
explanation: Because countries differ in population size, income per person, or per capita income, gives a fairer basis for comparing living standards across nations than total national income.

subject: Economics
text: The speculative demand for money is inversely related to the
explanation: Keynes argued that when interest rates are high, people prefer to hold interest-bearing bonds rather than idle cash, so speculative money demand falls as interest rates rise, and vice versa.

subject: Economics
text: If Mr. K obtains a ₦50,000 loan from a bank for the purpose of providing household needs, the demand for money is said to be
explanation: Money demanded to meet everyday spending needs, such as household expenses, represents the transactionary or transactions motive for holding money.

subject: Economics
text: Which of the following is used by the Central Bank of Nigeria to control inflation?
explanation: The discount, or interest, rate is a core monetary policy tool the central bank adjusts to influence borrowing, spending, and inflation, whereas tariffs and tax rates are fiscal tools.

subject: Economics
text: If CBN reduces money supply, the interest rate will
explanation: A reduction in money supply makes loanable funds scarcer relative to demand, pushing up the price of borrowing money, the interest rate.

subject: Economics
text: An example of an expansionary fiscal policy action is

options.A: decrease in the corporate profit tax rates

explanation: Expansionary fiscal policy involves the government cutting taxes or raising spending to boost aggregate demand; lowering corporate tax rates leaves firms more after-tax income to invest and spend.

subject: Economics
text: A tax on land will ultimately fall
explanation: Since the supply of land is fixed and perfectly inelastic, landowners cannot pass the tax on to tenants by reducing supply, so the full burden of a land tax falls on the owner.

subject: Economics
text: One of the goals of development plans in Nigeria is to

options.A: increase the profitability of multinational businesses
options.C: achieve higher standard of living for the citizens

explanation: The ultimate purpose of national development planning is to improve the wellbeing and living standards of citizens, not merely to raise abstract output figures.

subject: Economics
text: Life insurance companies contribute to economic development by holding a part of their assets in
explanation: Because life insurance liabilities are long-term, insurers typically invest premiums in long-term instruments like bonds and equities, channeling savings into long-term investment projects.

subject: Economics
text: The main reason for low agricultural produce in West Africa is

options.A: the presence of large-scale agro-allied industries
options.C: over dependence on agriculture for subsistence
options.D: the use of crude implements in the farming process

explanation: Much of West African farming still relies on simple hand tools rather than modern mechanized equipment, which limits how much land can be cultivated and how much can be produced.

subject: Economics
text: The most important determinant for the location of a brick industry is the availability of
explanation: Brick-making depends directly on clay and similar raw materials, so brick factories are usually sited close to their raw material deposits to minimize transport costs.

subject: Economics
text: In Nigeria, efficiency in public corporations can be achieved through
explanation: Transferring ownership of public corporations to private investors, or privatization, introduces profit incentives and market discipline, which tend to improve efficiency compared to state ownership.

subject: Economics
text: Government participation in the oil industry was necessitated by the
explanation: Oil exploration and production require enormous capital investment, which prompted the Nigerian government to take an equity stake alongside multinational oil companies.

subject: Economics
text: The deregulation of the petroleum sector in Nigeria will bring about

options.A: efficiency in pricing and distribution of the products
options.B: an end to the importation of fuel
options.C: an end to foreign firms' dominance

explanation: Removing government price controls allows market forces to determine petroleum prices, which is expected to improve efficiency in how fuel is priced and distributed.

subject: Economics
text: A distinguishing characteristic of consumer co-operative society is that the
explanation: A defining feature of a consumer co-operative is that the customers themselves are also the members or owners of the society, sharing in its control and profits.

subject: Economics
text: The quality of labour force in Nigeria can be improved by

options.C: encouraging the study of science and technology

explanation: Skills acquisition centres provide hands-on, practical vocational training that directly equips workers with employable skills, raising the overall quality of the labour force.

subject: Economics
text: The effect of emigration on a country's population is
explanation: Emigration means people leaving a country to live elsewhere, which directly reduces the resident population of the country they leave.

subject: Economics
text: One of the main achievements of the Economic Commission for Africa is

options.C: guaranteeing a steady flow of foreign investment into Africa
options.D: providing the machinery for collaboration on monetary issues

explanation: The ECA has worked to promote regional economic cooperation, including efforts to reduce trade barriers among African states to boost intra-African trade.

subject: Economics
text: The rate of interest charged on loans depends largely on

options.C: the risk associated with the loan

explanation: Lenders charge higher interest rates for riskier loans to compensate for the greater chance of default, making risk a major determinant of the interest rate charged.

subject: Economics
text: A valid explanation for real wage growth is

options.A: an increase in the rate of productivity
options.B: the rising cost of capital accumulation
options.C: a contraction of employment in service industries
options.D: an increase in the quantity of labour

explanation: Real wages tend to rise sustainably when workers become more productive, since firms can afford to pay more when output per worker increases.

subject: Economics
text: If Mr. X lost his clerical job at a store and searched for a similar job for ten months before finding one, this implies that Mr. X was
explanation: Frictional unemployment refers to the temporary joblessness that occurs while a worker searches for a new job matching their skills, which fits Mr. X's situation.

subject: Economics
text: The choice of how to produce in a command economy is determined by
explanation: In a command (centrally planned) economy, the government makes the key economic decisions, including how goods should be produced, rather than leaving it to market forces.

subject: Economics
text: The best measure of dispersion to determine the tallest tree in a forest is
explanation: To find the tallest tree, one only needs the difference between the tallest and shortest tree, which is exactly what the range measures.

subject: Economics
text: A change in demand for a normal good implies that there is a

options.A: change in the quantity demanded as price changes
options.C: movement along a given demand curve
options.D: change in the price elasticity of demand

explanation: A change in demand, as opposed to a change in quantity demanded, refers to a shift of the entire demand curve, caused by factors other than the good's own price, such as income or tastes.

subject: Economics
text: Given the table: at a price of ₦8 the quantity supplied is 20 units, and at a price of ₦10 the quantity supplied is 24 units. Calculate the price elasticity of supply
explanation: Percentage change in quantity supplied is (24-20)/20, which is 20%, and percentage change in price is (10-8)/8, which is 25%. Elasticity is 20 divided by 25, which is 0.8.

subject: Economics
text: When price is set below equilibrium, this will lead to

options.A: an increase in the quantity supplied
options.C: a decrease in the quantity supplied

explanation: At a price below equilibrium, producers are willing to supply less while consumers want to buy more, so quantity supplied falls relative to the equilibrium level, causing a shortage.

subject: Economics
text: Price mechanism determines the prices of commodities through
explanation: Under the price mechanism, prices are set by the free interaction of demand and supply, market forces, rather than by government decree.

subject: Economics
text: If the production of a large firm is higher than that of a small firm due to its own internal organization, it is experiencing
explanation: When a firm's own growth and efficient internal organization allow it to produce more at lower average cost, this reflects internal economies of scale, as opposed to benefits arising from the industry's growth.

subject: Economics
text: Division of labour requires that the tasks in a production line be performed
explanation: Division of labour means breaking production into separate tasks, each performed by workers who specialize in and become skilled at that particular task.

subject: Economics
text: Given that FC = ₦500, VC = ₦1,500, and Q = 50 units, find the average cost of the product
explanation: Total cost equals fixed cost plus variable cost, which is 500 plus 1,500, or ₦2,000. Average cost is total cost divided by quantity, which is 2,000 divided by 50, or ₦40.

subject: Economics
text: Rent and administrative expenses are examples of
explanation: Rent and administrative expenses generally do not change with the level of output in the short run, making them fixed costs.

subject: Economics
text: A perfect competitor will continue to expand output up to the point where
explanation: A firm keeps expanding output as long as an extra unit adds more to revenue than to cost, that is while MC is less than MR; it stops exactly where MC equals MR, the profit-maximizing point.

subject: Economics
text: One of the characteristics of a monopolist is that he can influence
explanation: Unlike a perfectly competitive firm, a monopolist is a price-maker who can choose either the price to charge or the quantity to sell, effectively controlling both.

subject: Economics
text: Which of the following can be used to measure the Gross National Product in an open economy?
explanation: GNP in an open economy equals consumption, investment, and government spending, plus net exports, which is exports minus imports, giving C+I+G+(X-M).

subject: Economics
text: If MPC = 2/3 and investment is ₦100 million, the level of national income is
explanation: The multiplier is 1/(1-MPC) = 1/(1-2/3) = 3. Multiplying by the ₦100 million investment gives a rise in national income of ₦300 million.

subject: Economics
text: The precautionary demand for money is determined by
explanation: Precautionary money balances are held to meet unforeseen expenses, and the amount people set aside for this depends mainly on their level of income.

subject: Economics
text: An inflation that co-exists with a high rate of unemployment is
explanation: Stagflation describes the unusual and troubling combination of stagnant economic growth, that is high unemployment, alongside rising prices, or inflation.

subject: Economics
text: Short-term loans for investment are usually obtained through the
explanation: The money market specializes in short-term borrowing and lending instruments such as treasury bills and commercial paper, ideal for short-term financing needs.

subject: Economics
text: Given a base year and a price index of 175% the following year, which of the following will arise?

options.A: The cost of living decreases that year
options.B: The cost of living remains unchanged
options.C: The value of money rises by 75%
options.D: The value of money falls by 75%

explanation: A price index of 175, from a base of 100, means prices rose by 75%, meaning each unit of money now buys 75% less, so the value or purchasing power of money has fallen accordingly.

subject: Economics
text: Wage freeze is a policy measure aimed at
explanation: By preventing wages from rising, a wage freeze helps control the growth of production costs and consumer spending power, both of which can fuel inflation.

subject: Economics
text: A major obstacle to the development of Nigeria's economy is
explanation: Nigeria's heavy reliance on oil exports for revenue exposes the economy to global oil price shocks and has slowed diversification into other productive sectors.

subject: Economics
text: A major feature of an underdeveloped economy is

options.C: low level of standard of living

explanation: Underdeveloped economies are typically characterized by low income per person, which directly translates into a low general standard of living for citizens.

subject: Economics
text: An important role of agriculture in Nigeria's economic development is the

options.A: processing of raw materials for industries

explanation: Agriculture remains the largest employer of labour in Nigeria, especially in rural areas, making job or employment provision one of its most important roles.

subject: Economics
text: A major disadvantage of localization of industry is
explanation: When many firms of the same industry cluster in one area, a downturn in that industry can throw many workers there out of work at once, since their skills are not easily transferable, causing structural unemployment.

subject: Economics
text: One major factor that determines the location of an industry is

options.D: the social responsibility of the firm

explanation: Firms often locate close to their main markets to reduce transportation costs and delivery time for their finished goods.

subject: Economics
text: The major contribution of OPEC to the Nigerian economy is the

options.B: granting of subsidies on petroleum products

explanation: As a member of OPEC, Nigeria benefits from the organization's efforts to coordinate output among oil-producing countries, which helps stabilize world oil prices and revenue.

subject: Economics
text: The loading of crude oil at the terminal is an activity in the

options.A: downstream sector of the oil industry
options.B: upstream and downstream sector of the oil industry
options.C: upstream sector of the oil industry
options.D: midstream and upstream sectors of the oil industry

explanation: Loading crude oil for export is part of the extraction and initial handling stage, which falls under the upstream sector of the petroleum industry.

subject: Economics
text: The short-run average variable cost of a firm will rise owing to

options.C: an increase in the cost of labour
options.D: an increase in the salaries of directors

explanation: Labour is a variable input used more intensively as output rises; a rise in wage costs directly increases the average variable cost of production.

subject: Economics
text: Firms embark on vertical integration in order to

options.A: take over markets formerly controlled by other firms
options.B: provide employment opportunities for the people
options.C: estimate a country's per capita income

explanation: Vertical integration involves a firm acquiring or merging with businesses at different stages of its supply chain, allowing it to control markets and processes previously handled by other firms.

subject: Economics
text: An accurate census figure is an important tool for planners in
explanation: Knowing exactly how many people live where allows planners to allocate resources like schools, hospitals, and infrastructure efficiently to match population needs.

subject: Economics
text: An accurate population census is important in order to

options.A: produce more food for the growing population
options.B: provide employment opportunities for the people
options.C: estimate a country's per capita income

explanation: Per capita income is calculated by dividing national income by population; an accurate census is essential to get a reliable population figure for this calculation.

subject: Economics
text: A major determinant of floating exchange rate is

options.A: the highest denomination of the currency
options.D: the forces of demand and supply

explanation: Under a floating exchange rate regime, the value of a currency is left to be determined by market forces of demand and supply for that currency, without central bank fixing.

subject: Economics
text: If the importation of a commodity is limited to a definite quantity, the trade control measure imposed is
explanation: A quota is a trade restriction that limits the physical quantity of a good that may be imported, unlike a tariff or duty, which is a tax on imports.

subject: Economics
text: The main objective of WTO is to

options.A: ensure adequate protection of infant industries
options.B: provide a mechanism for tariff reduction
options.C: assist countries with chronic balance of payments problems
options.D: assist developing countries to execute developmental projects

explanation: The WTO's founding purpose is to negotiate and enforce rules that progressively reduce tariffs and other trade barriers among member countries.

subject: Economics
text: The major determinant of the total volume of output in an economy is the
explanation: Total output responds to overall aggregate demand, or spending, in the economy; when total expenditure rises, firms produce more to meet it, and vice versa.

subject: Economics
text: The labour force of a country is determined by the

options.D: number of people available for work

explanation: The labour force specifically consists of people who are of working age and are either employed or actively seeking work, that is, those available for work.

subject: Economics
text: The major implication of high dependency ratio on an economy is a
explanation: A high dependency ratio means many non-working dependents rely on relatively few workers, reducing savings and investment per worker and reinforcing a cycle of low income and poverty.

subject: Economics
text: One of the major problems of the agricultural sector in Nigeria is
explanation: Much of Nigerian farming remains manual and small-scale because farmers lack access to modern tools and machinery, limiting productivity.

subject: Economics
text: The marginal propensity to save measures the

options.A: ratio of change in saving to a change in income
options.B: change in supply as a result of a change in consumption
options.C: ratio of change in consumption to a change in the level of income

explanation: MPS is defined as the fraction of any additional unit of income that a household chooses to save rather than spend, that is, the change in saving divided by the change in income.

subject: Economics
text: One of the major problems of census in Nigeria is
explanation: Census exercises in Nigeria have often been marred by controversy over inflated or manipulated figures, especially due to their link to political representation and revenue allocation.

subject: Economics
text: A decrease in government expenditure in an economy will cause the general price level to
explanation: Lower government spending reduces aggregate demand in the economy; with less total spending chasing goods and services, the general price level tends to fall.

subject: Economics
text: Given the table of a unit of labour, total product, average product, and marginal product: 1 worker gives total product 6; 2 workers give total product 14 with marginal product 8; 3 workers give total product K with average product 9 and marginal product 13; 4 workers give total product 40 with average product 10 and marginal product F. Find the values of K and F respectively
explanation: Since average product equals total product divided by labour, the 3rd worker's average product of 9 with 3 workers gives a total product of 27, which is K. The marginal product of the 4th worker is 40 minus 27, which equals 13, giving F.

subject: Economics
text: In the theory of consumer behaviour, the sum of all marginal utilities is
explanation: Total utility is built up by adding together the marginal utility gained from each successive unit consumed.

subject: Economics
text: In a pure capitalist economy, the means and forces of production are owned and controlled by the
explanation: Capitalism is defined by private ownership of the means of production, with individuals and firms, not the state, controlling resources and making production decisions.

subject: Economics
text: The demand for money is referred to as the

options.A: amount of money in fixed deposit
options.B: desire to hold money in intangible assets
options.D: desire to hold money in liquid form rather than investing it

explanation: Demand for money in economics refers to people's preference to hold their wealth as ready cash, or liquidity, rather than in interest-earning assets, for reasons like transactions, precaution, or speculation.

subject: Economics
text: In the theory of production, a producer is at equilibrium at a point where

options.A: isocost is above the isoquant curve
options.B: isoquant is below the isocost curve
options.C: isocost is tangent to the marginal revenue
options.D: isoquant is tangent to the isocost curve

explanation: A producer minimizes cost for a given output, or maximizes output for a given cost, exactly where an isoquant is tangent to an isocost line.

subject: Economics
text: Balance of trade shows the relationship between the

options.B: total value of visible import and export
options.C: deficit and surplus balance of payments
options.D: total value of invisible import and export

explanation: The balance of trade specifically compares the value of a country's visible, physical goods, exports against its visible imports, unlike the broader balance of payments.

subject: Economics
text: If the cash reserve ratio is 30%, a new deposit of ₦20 million will increase money supply by
explanation: The credit multiplier is 1 divided by the cash reserve ratio, which is 1/0.3, about 3.33. Applying this to the ₦20 million initial deposit gives a total potential increase in money supply of about ₦66.7 million.

subject: Economics
text: Payment of interest on loans and the repayment of the capital sum at a future date is
explanation: Debt servicing refers to the ongoing obligation of paying both interest and principal on a loan according to an agreed repayment schedule.

subject: Economics
text: The most difficult measure of central tendency to determine in a grouped data is
explanation: Unlike the mean, which can be computed directly from class midpoints and frequencies, the mode in grouped data requires estimation using a special interpolation formula, making it comparatively harder to pin down.

subject: Economics
text: Localization of industry is mainly determined by
explanation: Industries often cluster together in a particular location mainly because that location offers convenient access to the raw materials they need.

subject: Economics
text: A tax burden on a commodity will be shared equally if the demand is
explanation: When the price elasticity of demand equals the price elasticity of supply, as with unitary elastic demand relative to a typical supply curve, the tax burden tends to be split evenly between buyers and sellers.

subject: Economics
text: Higher income taxes can be used to control
explanation: Raising income tax reduces households' disposable income and spending power, which helps curb the excess demand that drives demand-pull inflation.

subject: Economics
text: An indifference map is made up of a set of
explanation: An indifference map is simply a collection of indifference curves, each showing combinations of two goods that give the consumer the same level of satisfaction, at different utility levels.

subject: Economics
text: Recapitalization policy will enable commercial banks to

options.D: have a very strong capital base

explanation: Recapitalization requires banks to raise their minimum share capital, directly strengthening their financial base and ability to absorb losses and fund larger transactions.

subject: Economics
text: The number of persons required to form a private company ranges from
explanation: Under company law as historically applied in Nigeria, a private limited liability company can be formed with a minimum of two and a maximum of fifty members.

subject: Economics
text: The major function of the International Monetary Fund is the

options.A: provision of short-run loans to medium scale industries
options.B: provision of long-term loans for infrastructure
options.C: development of agriculture in member countries

explanation: The IMF was established mainly to promote international monetary cooperation and exchange rate stability among member countries, providing short-term support to countries facing balance of payments problems.

subject: Economics
text: The law of supply states that the

options.A: higher the price the higher the quantity supplied
options.B: quantity supplied is always equal to the quantity demanded
options.C: lower the price the higher the quantity supplied
options.D: higher the price the lower the quantity supplied

explanation: The law of supply describes a direct, positive relationship between price and quantity supplied - as price rises, producers are willing to supply more, all else equal.

subject: Economics
text: One of the features of a perfectly competitive firm is
explanation: Perfect competition assumes a homogeneous market with no transport cost differences between sellers, ensuring buyers face the same effective price everywhere.

subject: Economics
text: The basis for international trade is the

options.C: prevailing interest rate on multilateral loans

explanation: Countries trade because they are endowed with different natural resources, climates, and factors of production, giving each a comparative advantage in producing certain goods.

subject: Economics
text: Training farmers on improved farming techniques is the major activity of
explanation: Agricultural extension workers are specifically tasked with teaching farmers modern, improved methods of cultivation to boost productivity.

subject: Economics
text: In a free market economy, prices are determined by

options.D: the forces of demand and supply

explanation: In a free, unregulated market, neither government nor any single group fixes prices; they emerge naturally from the interaction of buyers' demand and sellers' supply.

subject: Economics
text: The basic economic problem of what to produce in an economy is determined by
explanation: What an economy can produce is fundamentally constrained by the resources such as land, labour, and capital that it has available, which determine the range of possible output choices.

subject: Economics
text: In Nigeria, a fall in the supply of petrol would generally affect the

options.C: general price level in the country

explanation: Since petrol powers transportation and many production processes, a shortage raises transport and production costs across the economy, pushing up the general price level.

subject: Economics
text: The short-run equilibrium point of a perfectly competitive firm is attained at a point where

options.A: it is equal to total cost
options.B: the demand curve is tangential to the marginal cost
options.C: the marginal cost curve is equal to the marginal revenue curve
options.D: the marginal cost is equal to the price of the firm

explanation: A profit-maximizing firm produces up to the point where the cost of producing one more unit, marginal cost, exactly equals the revenue from selling it, marginal revenue.

subject: Economics
text: If a fall in the price of commodity K brought about an increase in the demand for commodity Y, the two commodities are
explanation: When a fall in the price of K causes demand for Y to rise rather than fall, consumers are switching from Y to the now-cheaper K, showing that K and Y are substitute goods.

subject: Government
text: Nation-state is synonymous with
explanation: A nation-state combines a people (nation) with an independent government exercising supreme authority over a territory. That supreme, self-governing authority is called sovereignty, which is the defining attribute of statehood.

subject: Government
text: A fundamental component of political culture is
explanation: Political culture is the pattern of attitudes, beliefs and values a people hold toward their political system. Shared social values shape how citizens view authority, participation and legitimacy, making them a fundamental component of political culture.

subject: Government
text: A state that is ruled by an elected citizen is
explanation: A republic is a state in which supreme power rests with the people and their elected representatives, headed by an elected president rather than a hereditary monarch. Because leadership comes through election, not birth, it is a republic.

subject: Government
text: A true democracy in the modern sense exists where the
explanation: In the modern (representative) sense, direct rule by all citizens is impractical, so democracy operates through elected representatives who govern on the people's behalf. This is why modern democracies are described as representative rather than direct.

subject: Government
text: In a parliamentary system, when the legislature passes a vote of no confidence on the executive, it means that the

options.A: executive is expected to go on suspension
options.B: legislature ceases to trust the executive
options.D: legislature commences legal proceeding against the executive

explanation: In a parliamentary system the executive holds office only as long as it enjoys the confidence of the legislature. A successful vote of no confidence withdraws that support, obliging the government (cabinet) to resign or seek a fresh mandate through dissolution.

subject: Government
text: The legislative body of the United States of America is the
explanation: The bicameral federal legislature of the United States is called Congress, made up of the Senate and the House of Representatives. 'Parliament' is used in the UK and 'National Assembly' in some other countries.

subject: Government
text: Unicameralism is a feature of the legislature in
explanation: A unicameral legislature has only one chamber. Israel's legislature, the Knesset, is a single-chamber house, whereas the United States and the United Kingdom operate bicameral (two-chamber) legislatures.

subject: Government
text: The upper house in most federal systems is created to

options.C: oversee and check the lower house
options.D: enable experienced elders make inputs to governance

explanation: In a federation the upper house (e.g. the Senate) usually gives each federating unit equal representation regardless of its size or population. This protects smaller units from being dominated by larger ones in the lower house, which is based on population.

subject: Government
text: In which of the following systems is the power of the component units more than that of the central government?
explanation: In a confederation the component (member) states retain most sovereignty and delegate only limited powers to a weak central body, which they can override or leave. Thus the units are more powerful than the centre, unlike in a federation where power is shared, or a unitary state where the centre is supreme.

subject: Government
text: One of the general tenets of fascist doctrine is that the leader is

options.C: subordinate to the laws of the state
options.D: subordinate to the norms of the society

explanation: Fascism glorifies the state and an all-powerful leader whose will stands above law and constitution. The leadership principle (Fuhrerprinzip) places the leader as supreme, unbound by constitutional limits.

subject: Government
text: In a cabinet system of government, executive power is exercised by the
explanation: The cabinet (parliamentary) system fuses executive and legislature, and real executive power is exercised by the head of government, the prime minister, and the cabinet. The monarch or ceremonial president is only a titular head of state.

subject: Government
text: The principle of separation of powers is best practiced in the
explanation: Separation of powers keeps the executive, legislature and judiciary distinct in personnel and function. This is best realised in the presidential system, where the president and legislators are separately elected, unlike the parliamentary system which fuses executive and legislature.

subject: Government
text: A typical form of delegated legislation is
explanation: Delegated (subsidiary) legislation is law made by a body other than the sovereign legislature, using powers granted by a parent Act. A bye-law made by a local council is a classic example; acts and bills are products of the legislature itself.

subject: Government
text: The rights of a citizen can be withdrawn by the state if the person

options.C: is convicted of a serious crime

explanation: Citizens' rights are not absolute; the state may lawfully curtail some rights (such as liberty or the franchise) when a person is convicted of a serious crime. Conviction after due process is a recognised legal ground for restricting rights.

subject: Government
text: An electoral process in which candidates are selected for elective offices by party members is
explanation: A primary election is an intra-party contest in which members choose the candidates who will represent the party in the main election. A general election is between parties, while a bye-election fills a vacancy that arises mid-term.

subject: Government
text: In theory one major advantage of the one-party system is that it

options.B: serves as an instrument of national integration
options.C: promotes greater mass participation in government

explanation: Supporters of the one-party system argue that a single party can unify diverse ethnic and regional groups behind common national goals, serving as an instrument of national integration. This was a common justification in newly independent African states.

subject: Government
text: A tactic employed by pressure groups to achieve their objectives is
explanation: Pressure groups seek to influence government without contesting elections. Lobbying, directly persuading legislators and officials, is a principal tactic they use to advance their members' interests.

subject: Government
text: Public opinion can be measured through
explanation: A referendum is a direct vote in which the electorate expresses its view on a specific issue, giving a reliable measure of public opinion. Rumour and strikes are not systematic measures of what the public thinks.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following is the main function of the civil service?

options.B: Allocating resources to the federating units
options.D: Mobilizing grass root support for government

explanation: The civil service is the permanent, non-political administrative arm that carries out the decisions of the government of the day. Its core function is implementing (executing) government policies, remaining neutral regardless of which party is in power.

subject: Government
text: Who was the political head of the Old Oyo Empire?
explanation: The Alaafin was the supreme political and spiritual head of the Old Oyo Empire. The Oyomesi was the council of kingmakers headed by the Bashorun, who checked the Alaafin's powers.

subject: Government
text: The Igbo political system was based on
explanation: Traditional Igbo society was largely acephalous (stateless), governed by village assemblies and institutions such as age grades that shared administrative, judicial and defence duties. This decentralised, participatory arrangement made autocracy difficult.

subject: Government
text: France introduced the policy of assimilation in her colonies primarily to

options.A: teach them the art of leadership

explanation: The French policy of assimilation aimed to turn colonial subjects into 'black Frenchmen' by imposing French language, culture, law and values. Its primary goal was therefore to change the colonised peoples' way of life.

subject: Government
text: The foremost British trading company on the West African coast was
explanation: The Royal Niger Company, chartered in 1886 under George Goldie, was the foremost British trading company on the West African coast and administered the Niger territories until its charter was revoked in 1900.

subject: Government
text: One major weakness of the Independence Constitution is that it

options.A: failed to provide the country with full sovereignty
options.C: gave full powers to the Supreme Court in Nigeria
options.D: empowered Britain to continue to rule

explanation: Under the 1960 Independence Constitution the British monarch remained Nigeria's head of state and the Judicial Committee of the Privy Council remained the final court of appeal. Nigeria therefore lacked full sovereignty until it became a republic in 1963.

subject: Government
text: The first law-making body in Nigeria after amalgamation was the
explanation: After the 1914 amalgamation, Lord Lugard established the Nigerian Council in 1914 as an advisory law-making body. It preceded the later Legislative Council introduced by the Clifford Constitution of 1922.

subject: Government
text: The designation of ministers as chief executives and accounting officers was recommended by a commission headed by
explanation: The 1988 Civil Service Reforms, based on the Dotun Philips panel, professionalised ministries and made ministers the chief executives and accounting officers of their ministries. This shifted accounting responsibility away from permanent secretaries.

subject: Government
text: A major shortcoming of the Ombudsman is

options.C: its inability to restrain bureaucratic excesses
options.D: lack of executive power to enforce decisions

explanation: The Ombudsman (in Nigeria, the Public Complaints Commission) investigates administrative injustice but can only recommend remedies. Its major weakness is that it has no executive power to enforce its findings against erring officials.

subject: Government
text: One of the strong points of the multiparty Nigeria's Fourth Republic is

options.A: the provision for a bicameral legislature

explanation: A multiparty system offers voters real choice and allows more interests and groups to organise and compete. Its strength is therefore wider political participation and representation of diverse views.

subject: Government
text: In which of the following is the ceremonial and executive powers fused?
explanation: In a presidential system the president is simultaneously head of state (ceremonial functions) and head of government (executive functions). These two roles are fused in one office, unlike the parliamentary system where a monarch or ceremonial president is separate from the prime minister.

subject: Government
text: A major contentious issue confronting Nigerian federalism is
explanation: Revenue allocation, how national income is shared among the federal, state and local governments and on what principles (derivation, population, need), is the most persistent source of conflict in Nigerian federalism.

subject: Government
text: The main purpose of establishing public enterprises in Nigeria is to
explanation: Public enterprises (parastatals) were set up mainly to provide essential goods and services, such as water, power, transport and communications, that the private sector could not or would not supply affordably. Profit-making is secondary to this social purpose.

subject: Government
text: The highest organ of the state during the Babangida Regime was the
explanation: General Ibrahim Babangida (1985-1993) governed through the Armed Forces Ruling Council (AFRC) as the supreme decision-making body. The Supreme Military Council belonged to earlier regimes and the Provisional Ruling Council to the Abacha/Abubakar era.

subject: Government
text: Decree 34 of 1966 was unacceptable to many Nigerians because it was

options.A: seen as an instrument of impoverishment
options.B: perceived to abolish the federal system
options.C: promulgated without consultation with the people

explanation: The Unification Decree No. 34 of 1966, promulgated by General Aguiyi-Ironsi, abolished the federal structure and imposed a unitary system. It was fiercely resented, especially in the North, because it was seen as abolishing federalism.

subject: Government
text: Nigeria broke diplomatic relations with France in 1961 because of

options.A: France's atomic test in the Sahara Desert
options.B: General de-Gaulle's negative attitude towards her
options.C: her poor relations with the Francophone countries

explanation: Nigeria severed diplomatic ties with France in January 1961 in protest against France's atomic bomb tests in the Sahara Desert. This early stand reflected Nigeria's Africa-centred and anti-colonial foreign policy.

subject: Government
text: Nigeria spearheaded the formation of ECOWAS during the regime of
explanation: The Economic Community of West African States was established by the Treaty of Lagos in 1975, championed by General Yakubu Gowon of Nigeria alongside President Eyadema of Togo. Hence ECOWAS was formed during Gowon's regime.

subject: Government
text: The head of Nigeria's foreign mission in a Commonwealth nation is known as a
explanation: Diplomatic representatives exchanged between Commonwealth member states are titled High Commissioners rather than Ambassadors. An Ambassador heads a mission to a non-Commonwealth country.

subject: Government
text: One major function of the Authority of Heads of State and Government of ECOWAS is to

options.D: prepare the budget of the Community

explanation: The Authority of Heads of State and Government is the supreme organ of ECOWAS. Among its functions is appointing the Executive Secretary (now President of the Commission), who heads the day-to-day administration.

subject: Government
text: The tenure of non-permanent members of the United Nations Security Council is
explanation: The UN Security Council has ten non-permanent members elected by the General Assembly for two-year terms. This contrasts with the five permanent members (P5), who hold their seats indefinitely.

subject: Government
text: The Secretary-General of the United Nations is appointed by the

options.B: General Assembly on the recommendation of the Security Council
options.C: permanent members of the Security Council on the recommendation of the General Assembly

explanation: Under Article 97 of the UN Charter, the Secretary-General is appointed by the General Assembly upon the recommendation of the Security Council. The recommendation is subject to the veto of the permanent members before the Assembly confirms the appointment.

subject: Government
text: The approval of treaties and agreements of the Economic Community of West African States is the responsibility of the

options.D: Assembly of Heads of State and Government

explanation: The Authority (Assembly) of Heads of State and Government is the highest ECOWAS organ and gives final approval to treaties and major agreements. The Council of Ministers only prepares and recommends matters to the Authority.

subject: Government
text: The development of attitudes and beliefs about a political system is
explanation: Political socialization is the lifelong process by which individuals acquire attitudes, beliefs and values about their political system through agents such as the family, school, media and peer groups. It is how a political culture is transmitted from one generation to the next.

subject: Government
text: A major weakness of confederation is
explanation: In a confederation the component states retain sovereignty and may lawfully leave the union. This makes the arrangement loose and unstable, with a strong tendency towards secession and disintegration.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following country is a unitary state?
explanation: In a unitary state all constitutional power is concentrated in one central government, which may create or dissolve lower units. Ghana operates such a unitary system, whereas Nigeria, India and the USA are federations.

subject: Government
text: Ending a session of parliament by royal proclamation means the
explanation: Prorogation is the formal ending of a session of parliament by proclamation, usually of the Crown, without dissolving the house. Dissolution, by contrast, ends the life of the parliament and triggers a general election.

subject: Government
text: A main feature of the parliamentary system is that

options.A: the executive consists of all party members
options.B: judges are drawn from the ruling party
options.C: electoral commissioners leave at the end of their tenure
options.D: the executive is appointed by the legislature

explanation: In the parliamentary (cabinet) system the executive is drawn from and appointed out of the legislature, with the prime minister and ministers being members of parliament. This fusion of executive and legislature is its defining feature.

subject: Government
text: In a socialist economy, private accumulation of wealth is
explanation: Socialism seeks collective or state ownership of the major means of production and aims at an egalitarian, classless society. Private accumulation of wealth is therefore prohibited or heavily curtailed, unlike in capitalism where it is encouraged.

subject: Government
text: The earliest classification of constitutions was the work of
explanation: Aristotle, in his 'Politics', made the earliest systematic classification of constitutions, dividing governments by who rules and in whose interest (monarchy, aristocracy, polity and their perversions). This makes him the pioneer of comparative constitutional study.

subject: Government
text: Constitutionalism refers to

options.A: the process of drafting a constitution
options.C: the process of operating a constitution

explanation: Constitutionalism is the principle that government power must be limited by, and exercised strictly in accordance with, the constitution. It emphasises the rule of law and strict adherence to constitutional restraints on those in power.

subject: Government
text: An advantage of delegated legislation is that

options.A: much time is saved in the process
options.B: technical issues are handled by experts
options.D: it hastens the implementation of policy

explanation: Delegated legislation lets the executive make detailed rules without going through the full, lengthy legislative process. Its major advantage is that it saves the parliament's limited time for broad policy matters.

subject: Government
text: One essential duty of a citizen to his state is to
explanation: While rights are what the citizen enjoys, duties are what the citizen owes the state. Paying taxes is an essential civic duty because it funds public services and the running of government.

subject: Government
text: Franchise in an electoral process means the

options.D: rights and duties of a citizen

explanation: The franchise (suffrage) is the legal right to vote in elections. Universal adult suffrage means that right is extended to all qualified adult citizens regardless of sex, wealth or status.

subject: Government
text: Pressure groups harmonize different individual concerns through
explanation: Interest aggregation is the process of combining and reconciling many individual demands into broader, workable positions. Pressure groups perform this by harmonising diverse member concerns into a common stand.

subject: Government
text: In pre-colonial Igboland, autocratic rule was made difficult by the

options.B: absence of a centralized system of authority

explanation: Pre-colonial Igbo society was acephalous, lacking a single, centralised ruler. Authority was diffused among family heads, elders, age grades and the village assembly, which made autocratic (one-man) rule practically impossible.

subject: Government
text: The Yoruba traditional system of government was
explanation: The Yoruba operated a centralised monarchical system headed by an Oba (such as the Alaafin or Ooni) who ruled with councils of chiefs. Although the councils checked the king, the system was fundamentally monarchical.

subject: Government
text: Under the pre-colonial Sokoto Caliphate system, the next in command to the Sultan was the
explanation: In the Sokoto Caliphate the Waziri (Vizier) was the chief adviser and head of administration, ranking next to the Sultan. The Alkali was a judge, while the Madawaki commanded the army.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following societies was classified as acephalous?
explanation: An acephalous (headless) society has no single central ruler. The Igbo are the classic Nigerian example, governing themselves through village assemblies, elders and age grades rather than a paramount king, unlike centralised Benin.

subject: Government
text: Indirect rule encouraged
explanation: By ruling through traditional authorities, indirect rule kept Nigerians conscious of colonial domination and produced a Western-educated elite who resented their exclusion from real power. This discontent ultimately encouraged the rise of nationalism.

subject: Government
text: The main achievement of the nationalists in Nigeria was
explanation: The nationalists' central goal was to end colonial rule and win self-government. Their main achievement was the political liberation (independence) of Nigeria in 1960, though economic dependence largely remained.

subject: Government
text: The presidential system of government was introduced in Nigeria with the Constitution of
explanation: Nigeria abandoned the Westminster parliamentary model of the First Republic and adopted an American-style presidential system under the 1979 Constitution, which ushered in the Second Republic. Later constitutions retained this presidential structure.

subject: Government
text: The Action Group crisis of 1962 led to the formation of the
explanation: The split in the Action Group between Chief Awolowo and his deputy Chief Akintola led Akintola's faction to form the United People's Party (UPP) in 1962. This deepened the political crisis in the Western Region.

subject: Government
text: Under the 1963 Republican Constitution, the power of judicial review was vested in the
explanation: Judicial review is the power of courts to determine whether laws and executive acts conform to the constitution. Under the 1963 Republican Constitution this power was vested in the Supreme Court as the final court of appeal.

subject: Government
text: The rules and regulations of the civil service are called
explanation: The conduct, discipline and procedures of civil servants are governed by a code known as the General Orders (now Public Service Rules). It is the standard rulebook regulating the civil service.

subject: Government
text: The Code of Conduct Bureau was essentially established to

options.B: protect the rights of public servants
options.C: enhance probity and accountability in public service
options.D: ensure the independence of the public service

explanation: The Code of Conduct Bureau receives asset declarations from public officers and enforces the code of conduct. Its essential aim is to curb corruption and abuse of office in public life.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following political parties was the first to be formed when the ban on politics was lifted in 1978?
explanation: When the ban on party politics was lifted in 1978 for the Second Republic, the National Party of Nigeria (NPN) was the first party to emerge and be registered. It went on to win the 1979 elections.

subject: Government
text: The principle of federal character was adopted in order to promote equitable allocation of

options.A: positions and appointments among people of various regions
options.B: appointments between the North and the South
options.C: opportunities between the males and females
options.D: revenue between groups in the country

explanation: The federal character principle, entrenched from the 1979 Constitution, seeks to reflect Nigeria's diversity in public appointments so that no section dominates. It ensures equitable spread of positions and appointments among people of the various states and regions.

subject: Government
text: The component units of the Nigerian Federation comprise

options.A: national assembly, military, police and civil service
options.C: federal, state, local government and federal capital territory
options.D: federal capital territory, national assembly, supreme court and civil service
