explanation: Nigeria's federation is made up of the federal government, the states, the local governments and the Federal Capital Territory, Abuja. These are the recognised tiers or component units of the federation.

subject: Government
text: In Nigeria, privatization and commercialization policies were introduced to

options.A: hand over the control of commercial ventures to citizens
options.B: increase the asset base of government
options.C: divest government major control of commercial ventures
options.D: allow government control of the private sector

explanation: Privatization and commercialization were adopted to reduce the state's dominance of the economy by transferring public enterprises wholly or partly to private hands. The aim was to divest government of major control of commercial ventures and improve efficiency.

subject: Government
text: An example of a public corporation in Nigeria is the
explanation: A public corporation is a state-owned body created by statute to provide a service, run on business lines but publicly owned. The Nigerian Television Authority (NTA) fits this description, unlike commissions or a private commercial bank.

subject: Government
text: Rhodesia was the former name of
explanation: Southern Rhodesia became independent as Zimbabwe in 1980 under Robert Mugabe after a long liberation struggle. 'Rhodesia' was named after the colonialist Cecil Rhodes.

subject: Government
text: The adoption of non-alignment as a principle of Nigeria's foreign policy was aimed at

options.A: promoting Nigeria's leadership aspiration in Africa
options.B: attaining equal status with the world powers
options.C: fulfilling a basic requirement for acceptance in the UN Security Council
options.D: insulating Nigeria against having to take sides in the Cold War

explanation: Non-alignment meant refusing to join either the Western (US-led) or Eastern (USSR-led) bloc during the Cold War. Nigeria adopted it to protect her independence of action and avoid being forced to take sides in the East-West rivalry.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following countries pioneered the idea of ECOWAS alongside Nigeria?
explanation: The ECOWAS treaty of 1975 was jointly championed by General Gowon of Nigeria and President Gnassingbe Eyadema of Togo. Togo therefore pioneered the community alongside Nigeria.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following international organizations were in existence before the Second World War?
explanation: The League of Nations was founded in 1920 after the First World War, well before the Second World War. The UN (1945), OAU (1963) and ECOWAS (1975) all came into being afterwards.

subject: Government
text: The organ of the United Nations responsible for the approval of its annual budget is the
explanation: Under the UN Charter, approval of the organisation's budget and apportionment of expenses among member states is a function of the General Assembly. It is the main deliberative and financial-approving organ of the UN.

subject: Government
text: The distinctive attribute of a state is the monopoly of
explanation: Following Max Weber, the state is defined by its monopoly of the legitimate use of force (violence) within a territory. Only the state may lawfully coerce, which distinguishes it from other associations.

subject: Government
text: State as a political entity refers to

options.A: an organized group within a definite territory
options.B: an association of men in a given society

explanation: A state is an organised political community occupying a definite territory, with a population, a government and sovereignty. It is therefore best described as an organised group of people within a defined territory.

subject: Government
text: In a democratic government, political sovereignty is vested in the
explanation: Political sovereignty in a democracy resides ultimately with the people, expressed through the electorate whose votes confer authority on those who govern. Legal sovereignty may rest in the legislature, but political sovereignty belongs to the voters.

subject: Government
text: A governmental system in which constitutional supremacy resides in the centre is
explanation: In a unitary system the central government holds supreme constitutional authority and any regional or local bodies exercise only powers it delegates. Constitutional supremacy thus resides at the centre.

subject: Government
text: A bill is a draft which is awaiting the consideration of the
explanation: A bill is a proposed law in draft form that must be debated and passed by the legislature before it can become an Act. It is therefore a draft awaiting the consideration of the legislature.

subject: Government
text: The private ownership of the means of production is a feature of
explanation: Capitalism is an economic system based on private ownership of the means of production and the operation of the profit motive in a free market. Socialism and communism, by contrast, favour collective or state ownership.

subject: Government
text: In a feudal system, the two major classes are the serfs and the
explanation: Feudal society was organised around land held in exchange for service, with the landowning lords at the top and the serfs who worked the land at the bottom. The two principal classes were therefore the lords and the serfs.

subject: Government
text: An example of a country with a flexible constitution is
explanation: A flexible constitution can be amended by the ordinary law-making process. Britain, whose largely unwritten constitution can be changed by a simple Act of Parliament, is the classic example, unlike the rigid, written US Constitution.

subject: Government
text: The rule of law is a negation of
explanation: The rule of law insists that everyone, including the government, is subject to the law and that power must be exercised within legal limits. It therefore opposes and negates arbitrary or absolute power.

subject: Government
text: Delegated legislation is made by bodies other than the
explanation: Delegated (subsidiary) legislation is law made under authority granted by the parliament to other bodies such as ministers, commissions or councils. It is therefore made by bodies other than the parliament itself.

subject: Government
text: The right of citizens to participate in the affairs of government of their country is called
explanation: Political rights are those that enable citizens to take part in the running of their state, such as the right to vote, be voted for, and hold public office. Participation in government is thus a political right.

subject: Government
text: The commission charged with the conduct of federal elections in Nigeria is
explanation: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) is the current body constitutionally responsible for organising and conducting federal and state elections in Nigeria. It replaced earlier bodies such as FEDECO and NECON.

subject: Government
text: An electoral system in which parties are assigned seats in the parliament commensurate to the number of votes polled is
explanation: Under proportional representation each party wins seats in proportion to its share of the total votes cast. This makes the legislature mirror the spread of public opinion more closely than winner-takes-all systems.

subject: Government
text: In the Hausa pre-colonial political system, a district was headed by
explanation: In the Hausa-Fulani emirate system a district was administered by a district head known as the Hakimi, who was responsible to the Emir. The Alkali was a judge and the Waziri the chief minister.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following ensured the practice of democracy in the pre-colonial Yoruba political system?
explanation: Although the Yoruba had a monarchy, the Alaafin's power was checked by the Oyomesi (council of chiefs) and the Ogboni society, which could even compel a bad king to abdicate. These checks and balances introduced a democratic element into the system.

subject: Government
text: Colonization of Africa was mainly motivated by
explanation: The scramble for and partition of Africa was driven above all by the search for raw materials, markets and profitable investment for the industrialising powers of Europe. Economic motives were therefore the main driving force of colonisation.

subject: Government
text: Two foreigners that directly aroused nationalist feelings among Nigerians are

options.C: Edward Blyden and John Payne Jackson

explanation: Edward Wilmot Blyden, through his writings on the African personality, and John Payne Jackson, through his fiery Lagos Weekly Record, stirred early nationalist consciousness in Nigeria. Both were foreigners of West Indian and Liberian background respectively.

subject: Government
text: Members of the Senate in Nigeria's First Republic were

options.C: nominated by regional and federal governments
options.D: nominated by the president of the house

explanation: In the First Republic the Senate was largely a nominated chamber; its members were nominated by the regional and federal governments rather than elected by universal suffrage. Direct election of senators came in later republics.

subject: Government
text: In Nigeria, the agency mainly responsible for the maintenance of internal peace and security is the
explanation: The Nigeria Police Force is the primary agency charged with maintaining internal law, order and security. The armed forces are mainly for external defence and are used internally only in aid of civil authority.

subject: Government
text: The National Assembly in Nigeria is primarily responsible for
explanation: As the legislature, the National Assembly (Senate and House of Representatives) is primarily responsible for making the laws of the federation. Executing laws is the work of the executive and interpreting them the work of the judiciary.

subject: Government
text: The three registered political parties at the inception of Nigeria's Fourth Republic were
explanation: At the start of the Fourth Republic in 1999 the three registered parties were the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), the Alliance for Democracy (AD) and the All Peoples Party (APP). The PDP won the presidency under Olusegun Obasanjo.

subject: Government
text: The Sharia legal system was first introduced in the Fourth Republic in
explanation: In 1999-2000 Zamfara State, under Governor Ahmed Sani Yerima, became the first state in the Fourth Republic to adopt full Sharia criminal law. Several other northern states later followed.

subject: Government
text: Workers in the public corporations are known as
explanation: Employees of public corporations and parastatals are classified as public servants, since they serve state-owned bodies. Only those working in the ministries and departments of the core government are strictly civil servants.

subject: Government
text: Military intervention in Nigeria arose from

options.A: perceived incapability of civilians to govern
options.C: the desire for a military government

explanation: The military justified their coups largely by pointing to corruption, election rigging and mismanagement by civilian politicians. The perceived incapability of civilians to govern well was the main pretext for military intervention.

subject: Government
text: The first institution introduced by the military to exercise legislative power was the
explanation: When the military first took over in 1966 they created the Supreme Military Council (SMC) as the highest body, which exercised both executive and legislative functions by decree. Later regimes used differently named councils.

subject: Government
text: The main focus of Nigeria's foreign policy since independence centres on
explanation: Since independence Nigeria has made Africa the centre-piece of her foreign policy, an Afrocentric stance that prioritises African unity, decolonisation and the welfare of the continent. Africa's interest is placed at the heart of external relations.

subject: Government
text: The country that championed decolonization in Africa was
explanation: Ghana, under Kwame Nkrumah, became independent in 1957 and led the drive for the liberation of the rest of Africa and for Pan-African unity. It therefore championed the decolonisation of the continent.

subject: Government
text: The structures of the African Union include

options.A: the court of justice, pan African congress and people's Assembly
options.B: the pan African parliament, the court of justice and the peace and security council
options.C: specialized technical commission, the court of justice and humanitarian board
options.D: people's Assembly, humanitarian board and the peace and security council

explanation: Among the organs of the African Union are the Pan-African Parliament, the Court of Justice, and the Peace and Security Council. These structures give the AU stronger legislative, judicial and security machinery than its predecessor, the OAU.

subject: Government
text: ECOMOG at the initial stage of its intervention in Liberia was perceived as
explanation: When the ECOWAS Monitoring Group (ECOMOG) first intervened in the Liberian civil war it was seen as a neutral peacekeeping force sent to stop the bloodshed. Some warring factions later accused it of taking sides.

subject: Government
text: The African leader mostly credited for spearheading the formation of the African Union is
explanation: Libya's leader Muammar Gaddafi was the most prominent champion of transforming the OAU into the African Union, which was launched in 2002. He pushed strongly for deeper continental unity.

subject: Government
text: The founding members of OPEC are

options.A: Algeria, Iran, Iraq, Saudi Arabia and Kuwait
options.B: Nigeria, Libya, Iraq and Saudi Arabia
options.C: Venezuela, Nigeria, Libya, Iran and Iraq
options.D: Saudi Arabia, Iran, Iraq, Kuwait and Venezuela

explanation: OPEC was founded in Baghdad in 1960 by five countries: Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia and Venezuela. Nigeria and others joined later, so the founders are the five listed in option D.

subject: Government
text: De jure sovereignty is acquired through
explanation: De jure sovereignty is legal or rightful authority recognised by law, as opposed to de facto sovereignty gained by force. It is acquired through and rests upon the law.

subject: Government
text: According to Aristotle, a form of government in which the few rule for the benefit of all is
explanation: In Aristotle's classification, aristocracy is the good form of rule by the few, in which the best-qualified govern in the interest of the whole community. Its perverted form, where the few rule for their own benefit, is oligarchy.

subject: Government
text: Rule by the old people is known as
explanation: Gerontocracy is government by elders or old people, from the Greek 'geron' (old man) and 'kratos' (rule). It was common in many traditional African societies where age conferred authority and wisdom.

subject: Government
text: As an executive, the commissioner is charged with the responsibility of
explanation: A state commissioner is a member of the executive arm and heads a ministry. His responsibility is to implement (execute) the policies and laws of the government, not to make them, which is the legislature's role.

subject: Government
text: Rule adjudication is a primary function of the
explanation: Rule adjudication means the settling of disputes and the application of law to particular cases. This is the primary function of the judiciary, which interprets the law and administers justice.

subject: Government
text: The judiciary controls the executive in a federal state through
explanation: Judicial review is the power of the courts to examine and, where necessary, nullify executive acts that violate the constitution or the law. Through it the judiciary checks and controls the executive in a federation.

subject: Government
text: One major advantage of the unitary system is that it tends to make government
explanation: Because all authority is concentrated in one central government, the unitary system avoids conflicts between tiers and speaks with a single voice. This tends to make government strong, unified and stable.

subject: Government
text: The presidential system differs from the parliamentary system of government in that

options.A: the principle of collective responsibility applies
options.B: executive and legislative powers are fused
options.C: powers of the three arms of government are merged
options.D: the tenure of office of the president is fixed

explanation: In the presidential system the president is elected for a fixed term and cannot easily be removed by the legislature except through impeachment. In the parliamentary system the prime minister's tenure depends on retaining the confidence of parliament, so it is not fixed.

subject: Government
text: A constitution that is difficult to amend is
explanation: A rigid constitution is one that can only be changed through a special, difficult procedure such as a referendum or a two-thirds legislative majority. This makes it hard to amend, unlike a flexible constitution alterable by ordinary law.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following constitutions is more suitable for centralization of political power?
explanation: An unwritten (largely uncodified) constitution concentrates authority in the central legislature, whose ordinary Acts can reshape the system without special restraints. This makes it more suitable for the centralisation of political power.

subject: Government
text: The act of transferring autonomous powers to subordinate agencies is
explanation: Devolution is the transfer of substantial, autonomous powers from a central authority to lower-level or regional bodies. Delegation, by contrast, grants only limited authority that can be recalled.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following types of citizenship cannot be withdrawn?
explanation: Citizenship by birth is the most secure form and generally cannot be revoked by the state. Citizenship acquired by naturalisation, registration or as an honour can be withdrawn under certain conditions.

subject: Government
text: A political party is different from a pressure group in its
explanation: A political party aims to capture and exercise governmental power by contesting elections, whereas a pressure group merely tries to influence government policy without seeking to govern. Their difference lies chiefly in this objective or aim.

subject: Government
text: The earliest nationalist activities in Nigeria were spearheaded by the
explanation: Early Nigerian nationalism was led by the Western-educated elite, lawyers, journalists and clergymen, who used newspapers and associations to demand reforms. This class produced the pioneer nationalists.

subject: Government
text: In Nigeria's First Republic, the prime minister was both the

options.A: head of state and commander-in-chief of the armed forces
options.B: commander-in-chief of the armed forces and party leader
options.C: head of state and party leader
options.D: head of government and a lawmaker

explanation: In the parliamentary First Republic the prime minister, Sir Abubakar Tafawa Balewa, was head of government and also a member of the legislature. The ceremonial head of state was the Governor-General/President.

subject: Government
text: The major external factor that promoted nationalism in Nigeria was
explanation: The Second World War exposed Nigerian ex-servicemen to ideas of freedom and self-determination and weakened the prestige of the colonial powers. This external event greatly stimulated nationalist agitation for independence.

subject: Government
text: Under the 1979 Constitution, statutory allocation of revenue to local government councils is the responsibility of the
explanation: The 1979 Constitution provided for statutory allocation of revenue from the Federation Account to local governments, an allocation determined by the federal legislature. This gave local government a guaranteed share of national revenue.

subject: Government
text: Under Nigeria's Second Republic, the Senate was under the leadership of
explanation: In the Second Republic (1979-1983) the Senate was presided over by its President, Dr Joseph Wayas. Godwin Ume-Ezeoke was Speaker of the House of Representatives at the time.

subject: Government
text: The party system practiced in Nigeria's Third Republic was
explanation: During the aborted Third Republic under Babangida, the government created and imposed only two parties, the SDP and the NRC. Nigeria therefore operated a two-party system in that period.

subject: Government
text: The highest policy-making body under the Gowon Regime was the
explanation: General Yakubu Gowon (1966-1975) ruled through the Supreme Military Council (SMC), which was the highest policy-making body of his regime. The AFRC and PRC belonged to later military governments.

subject: Government
text: Nigeria became a federation of thirty-six states during the era of
explanation: General Sani Abacha created six additional states in 1996, bringing the total to the present thirty-six states. Nigeria therefore became a 36-state federation during Abacha's regime.

subject: Government
text: A public corporation is managed by
explanation: A public corporation is run by a board of directors appointed by government, which sets policy, while a general manager handles day-to-day administration. Overall management responsibility rests with the board of directors.

subject: Government
text: The Provisional Ruling Council was the highest ruling body during the regime of
explanation: The Provisional Ruling Council (PRC) was the supreme governing body under General Sani Abacha (1993-1998) and later Abdulsalami Abubakar. Buhari used the SMC and Babangida the AFRC.

subject: Government
text: Nigeria's role in the African Union was most prominent during the regime of
explanation: Nigeria, under President Olusegun Obasanjo, played a leading role in transforming the OAU into the African Union in 2002 and in launching NEPAD. His regime therefore marked Nigeria's most prominent role in the AU.

subject: Government
text: The organ of the UN that promotes voluntary co-operation among member states in diverse areas is the
explanation: The Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC) coordinates the economic, social, cultural, educational and humanitarian work of the UN and its specialised agencies. It promotes voluntary co-operation among members across these diverse fields.

subject: Government
text: The main representative body of the United Nations is the
explanation: The General Assembly is the main deliberative and representative organ of the UN, in which all member states are represented and each has one vote. It is therefore the chief representative body of the organisation.

subject: Government
text: The AU differs from the OAU in having

options.D: no assembly of Heads of State

explanation: Unlike the OAU, which respected absolute sovereignty and non-interference, the AU has stronger organs such as the Peace and Security Council and provisions for intervention. It therefore has more effective mechanisms for enforcing its decisions.

subject: Government
text: Membership of a society is
explanation: A person is born into and lives within a society without choosing to join it, and cannot simply opt out of social membership. Membership of a society is therefore compulsory (mandatory), unlike membership of an association or club.

subject: Government
text: One basic feature of a monarchical form of government is that

options.A: the ruler has a fixed tenure
options.C: members of the executive are elected

explanation: A monarchy is headed by a king or queen who typically inherits the throne and reigns for life. Succession through heredity is therefore its basic feature, distinguishing it from a republic with elected leaders.

subject: Government
text: One main advantage of a bicameral legislature is that it

options.B: makes for quick deliberation during emergencies
options.D: is less cumbersome to pass bills

explanation: Having two chambers means each reviews the other's work, so hasty or ill-considered laws are checked and it is harder for one group to dominate. A bicameral legislature is therefore less easy to manipulate or rush.

subject: Government
text: The court that has ultimate power to interpret the constitution is the
explanation: The Supreme Court is the highest court in Nigeria and its decisions are final and binding on all other courts. It has the ultimate power to interpret the constitution and settle constitutional disputes.

subject: Government
text: Unitary system of government is more suitable to a country

options.A: with a relatively small area and a homogeneous population
options.C: that possesses a strong and modern army
options.D: with a robust and dynamic economy

explanation: A unitary system concentrates power at the centre and works best where there are few deep regional or ethnic divisions to accommodate. It suits a small country with a homogeneous population rather than a large, diverse one.

subject: Government
text: An example of a country ruled by a constitutional monarch is
explanation: A constitutional monarch reigns but does not rule, exercising ceremonial functions within limits set by the constitution. Morocco is such an example, having a king whose powers are defined by its constitution.

subject: Government
text: The development of a classless society is the goal of
explanation: Marxism holds that class struggle drives history and that after a proletarian revolution the state and class distinctions will wither away. The creation of a classless, egalitarian society is its ultimate goal.

subject: Government
text: Laws made by military governments at the state level are called
explanation: Under military rule, laws made by the federal military government are decrees, while those made by military governors at the state level are called edicts. Edicts are thus the state-level equivalent of decrees.

subject: Government
text: Citizenship is acquired by an alien through
explanation: An adult foreigner who satisfies conditions such as long residence and good character can become a citizen through naturalisation. This is the standard route by which an alien acquires citizenship, distinct from citizenship by birth.

subject: Government
text: The officer responsible for announcing the result of an election is known as the
explanation: The returning officer is the official charged with collating the votes and formally declaring and announcing the result of an election in a constituency. The presiding officer only manages voting at a single polling unit.

subject: Government
text: The ultimate aim of political parties is to

options.D: increase the political awareness of the electorate

explanation: A political party is an organised group that seeks to win control of government by contesting and winning elections. Its ultimate aim is therefore to acquire and exercise political power.

subject: Government
text: The main objective of pressure groups is to

options.A: serve as opposition to the government
options.B: promote the interest of political parties
options.C: influence legislation for the benefit of their members
options.D: protect the interest of the country against foreigners

explanation: Pressure groups do not contest for power but seek to shape public policy in favour of their members. Their main objective is to influence legislation and government decisions for the benefit of their members' interests.

subject: Government
text: The body that is responsible for the appointment, discipline, promotion and dismissal of civil servants is the
explanation: The Civil Service Commission is the constitutional body empowered to appoint, promote, discipline and dismiss civil servants. This insulates the service from partisan political control and preserves its neutrality.

subject: Government
text: In the pre-colonial Hausa political system, the Madawaki performed the function of
explanation: In the Hausa-Fulani emirate structure the Madawaki was the commander of the emir's cavalry and army. His role therefore corresponds to that of a Minister of Defence in modern terms.

subject: Government
text: The General Strike of 1945 was caused primarily by the

options.A: disparity in the criteria for employment
options.B: harshness in trade laws as it concerns the Africans
options.C: government's rejection of a demand for an increase of 50 percent in the cost of living allowance

explanation: The 1945 General Strike was triggered mainly by workers' demand for a fifty per cent increase in the cost-of-living allowance to cope with wartime inflation, which the government resisted. It became a landmark in Nigerian labour and nationalist history.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following nationalists was the founder of Nigeria's first political party?
explanation: Herbert Macaulay founded the Nigerian National Democratic Party (NNDP) in 1923, the first modern political party in Nigeria. He is widely regarded as the father of Nigerian nationalism.

subject: Government
text: The division of powers between the federal and regional governments into exclusive, concurrent and residual lists was done by the
explanation: The 1960 Independence Constitution formally distributed legislative powers between the centre and the regions into exclusive, concurrent and residual lists. This entrenched the federal division of powers in Nigeria.

subject: Government
text: The main function of the Federal Character Commission in Nigeria is

options.A: providing free social services to the citizens
options.B: ensuring fair representation of all states in the public service

explanation: The Federal Character Commission enforces the constitutional requirement that public appointments reflect the federal character of Nigeria. Its main function is to ensure fair and equitable representation of all states and groups in the public service.

subject: Government
text: The power of appointing the chairman of the Independent National Electoral Commission is vested in the
explanation: Under the constitution the President appoints the INEC chairman, subject to confirmation by the Senate. The power of appointment is therefore vested in the President.

subject: Government
text: The NCNC and the NPC facilitated the creation of the
explanation: In 1963 the NCNC and NPC coalition supported carving the Mid-West Region out of the Western Region, partly to weaken the Action Group's base. The Mid-West thus became the fourth region.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following was done during the Gowon administration to reduce regional structural imbalance in the federation?
explanation: In 1967 General Gowon abolished the four regions and created twelve states to correct the imbalance caused by the dominance of the large Northern Region. State creation was his key measure to reduce regional structural imbalance.

subject: Government
text: One feature of public corporations that was weakened by privatization is
explanation: Privatization transfers ownership and management of public enterprises to private hands, reducing state ownership and direction. The feature most weakened is therefore government control over the enterprises.

subject: Government
text: One of the main duties of the Local Government Service Commission is to

options.A: handle requests for the creation of more local governments
options.B: supervise and manage the personnel of a local government
options.D: create an enabling working environment for council workers

explanation: The Local Government Service Commission handles staff matters, appointing, promoting, disciplining and managing the personnel of local governments in a state. Its main duty is therefore the supervision and management of local government staff.

subject: Government
text: The Option A4 model was used in the conduct of the
explanation: Option A4 was the open-secret voting method in which voters queued behind candidates or their symbols, used in the 1993 elections conducted under Babangida. That poll, won by M.K.O. Abiola, was famously annulled.

subject: Government
text: The review of Nigerian foreign policy under the Murtala-Obasanjo regime was done by the
explanation: The comprehensive review of Nigeria's foreign policy during the Murtala/Obasanjo era was carried out by the panel headed by Professor Adebayo Adedeji. It reaffirmed Africa as the centre-piece of foreign policy.

subject: Government
text: The Technical Aid Corps was established during the regime of
explanation: The Directorate of Technical Aid Corps was set up in 1987 under General Ibrahim Babangida to send Nigerian professionals to serve in other developing countries, especially in Africa, the Caribbean and the Pacific. It is an instrument of Nigeria's Afrocentric diplomacy.

subject: Government
text: Nigeria is regarded as a frontline state because she

options.A: sent troops for peacekeeping in Somalia
options.B: sent policemen for peacekeeping in Namibia
options.C: assisted the liberation struggle in Southern Africa

explanation: Frontline states were African countries that actively supported the struggle against colonialism and apartheid in Southern Africa. Nigeria earned this status by giving strong material and diplomatic backing to those liberation movements.

subject: Government
text: The reason behind Nigeria's suspension from the Commonwealth in 1995 was
explanation: Nigeria was suspended from the Commonwealth in 1995 following the execution of Ken Saro-Wiwa and other Ogoni activists by the Abacha military regime. The suspension was a response to that political and human-rights situation.

subject: Government
text: The number of permanent members of the UN Security Council is
explanation: The UN Security Council has five permanent members (the P5), China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom and the United States, each holding the power of veto. Ten other members serve on a non-permanent, rotating basis.

subject: Government
text: Former colonies of Britain belong to the association known as the
explanation: The Commonwealth of Nations is a voluntary association made up mainly of Britain and its former colonies, cooperating on shared values and interests. Membership is largely rooted in a common British colonial heritage.

subject: Government
text: The tenure of an elected chairman of local government is determined by the

options.B: National Union of Local Government Employees

explanation: Local government is a matter within the competence of the states, and the tenure of elected council chairmen is fixed by state law. It is therefore determined by the State House of Assembly.

subject: Government
text: The 1976 Local Government Reforms made local government the
explanation: The 1976 Local Government Reforms established a uniform, single-tier system of elected councils and recognised local government as the third tier of government, after the federal and state levels. This gave local government a defined constitutional status.

subject: Government
text: The political party that originated from Jamiyyar Mutanen Arewa was the
explanation: The Northern People's Congress (NPC) grew out of the cultural organisation Jamiyyar Mutanen Arewa (Northern Peoples' Congress) in the early 1950s. Led by Ahmadu Bello, it became the dominant party in the North.

subject: Government
text: A major consequence of colonialism on Nigeria is

options.B: the attainment of equal status with Europe

explanation: Colonial rule tied Nigeria's economy to the export of raw materials and the import of manufactured goods from the metropolitan power. A lasting consequence was economic dependence on the former colonial masters.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following political parties contested the 1993 Presidential Election?
explanation: The 1993 presidential election was contested by the two government-created parties, the National Republican Convention (NRC) and the Social Democratic Party (SDP). The SDP's M.K.O. Abiola won the poll, which was later annulled.

subject: Government
text: A mode of production in which the resources of a community are pooled together for the general well-being of the people is called
explanation: Communalism is the traditional system in which land and other resources are owned collectively by the community and used for the common good of all members. It emphasises communal ownership and shared welfare.

subject: Government
text: Which of these international organizations was Nigeria a founding member of?
explanation: Nigeria was among the states that founded ECOWAS through the Treaty of Lagos in 1975 and played a leading role in its creation. She was not a founder of the UNO (1945) or NATO (1949).

subject: Government
text: An example of a civil right of a citizen is the right to
explanation: Civil rights protect the individual's personal liberty and property under the law, such as the rights to own property and to fair justice. The rights to vote and be voted for are political rights, not civil rights.

subject: Government
text: Public corporations are controlled by the legislature through the
explanation: The legislature exercises control over public corporations mainly by scrutinising and approving their annual budgets and estimates. This financial oversight is its principal means of holding them accountable.

subject: Government
text: The elective principle was first introduced in Nigeria by the
explanation: The Clifford Constitution of 1922 introduced the elective principle for the first time in Nigeria, allowing a limited number of elected seats in the Legislative Council for Lagos and Calabar. This opened the door to party politics.

subject: Government
text: The type of pressure group that champions the interest of the under-privileged is known as
explanation: Promotional (or attitude) pressure groups campaign for a cause or the welfare of others rather than the material interest of their own members. Groups defending the under-privileged, human rights or the environment fall into this category.

subject: Government
text: The organ of the AU that prepares for the meeting of the Assembly of Heads of State and Government is the

options.A: Commission of Mediation, Conciliation and Arbitration

explanation: The Council of (Executive) Ministers prepares the agenda and groundwork for the meetings of the Assembly of Heads of State and Government of the African Union. It coordinates and recommends matters for the Assembly's decision.

subject: Government
text: One of the features of a fascist government is that

options.C: the state defines the rights of individuals
options.D: sovereignty is identified with landed property

explanation: Fascism exalts the all-powerful state over the individual and tolerates no opposition. A key feature is that the state, not the individual, defines and grants whatever rights citizens may enjoy.

subject: Government
text: Abolition of civil liberty is an attribute of
explanation: Military governments rule by decree, suspend the constitution and often curtail fundamental rights such as free speech and association. The abolition or suspension of civil liberties is a common attribute of military rule.

subject: Government
text: Nigeria's foreign relation with Britain was strained during the Buhari Regime because

options.A: Britain tested an atomic bomb in the Sahara Desert
options.B: Britain refused to recognize the regime
options.C: Nigeria refused to export crude oil to Britain
options.D: Nigeria wanted to forcefully extradite Alhaji Umaru Dikko from Britain

explanation: In 1984 Nigerian agents attempted to abduct the former minister Umaru Dikko from London to face corruption charges. The bungled kidnap attempt badly strained relations between Nigeria and Britain under the Buhari regime.

subject: Government
text: The Chairman of the Federal Character Commission is appointed by the

options.A: Secretary to the Government of the Federation

explanation: The Chairman and members of the Federal Character Commission are appointed by the President, subject to Senate confirmation. The power of appointment therefore lies with the President.

subject: Government
text: The Babangida Regime re-established diplomatic ties with
explanation: Nigeria had broken relations with Israel in 1973 following the Arab-Israeli war and an OAU resolution. The Babangida administration restored full diplomatic ties with Israel in 1992.

subject: Government
text: The remote cause of the Action Group Crisis of 1962 was the
explanation: The 1962 Action Group crisis grew out of a bitter power struggle between Chief Obafemi Awolowo and his deputy Chief Ladoke Akintola. This personality clash among the party's leaders was its remote cause.

subject: Government
text: In the Hausa pre-colonial system, the officer in charge of fishing activities was the
explanation: In the Hausa administrative system titles reflected specific duties: Sarkin Ruwa (chief of water) supervised fishing and water resources, Sarkin Noma oversaw farming, and Sarkin Pawa the butchers. Fishing was the charge of the Sarkin Ruwa.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following assists the president in the formulation of foreign policies?
explanation: The Ministry of Foreign (External) Affairs is the government department that advises the president and coordinates the conduct and formulation of the country's foreign policy. It manages Nigeria's relations with other states and organisations.

subject: Government
text: Rule by divine right is a basis of
explanation: The doctrine of the divine right of kings held that the monarch derived authority directly from God and was answerable to no one on earth. This belief underpinned absolute monarchy.

subject: Government
text: The Structural Adjustment Programme was introduced under the
explanation: The Structural Adjustment Programme (SAP), adopted in 1986 to restructure and liberalise the economy, was introduced by the military government of General Ibrahim Babangida. It featured devaluation of the naira and removal of subsidies.

subject: Government
text: The political achievement of the United Nations is the promotion of
explanation: The primary purpose of the UN, set out in Article 1 of its Charter, is to maintain international peace and security. Its greatest political achievement lies in preventing conflicts and promoting peaceful settlement of disputes.

subject: Government
text: Universal Adult Suffrage permits all
explanation: Universal adult suffrage grants the right to vote to all qualified adult citizens without discrimination on grounds of sex, wealth, religion or status. The key word is 'qualified', since minors and other disqualified persons are excluded.

subject: Government
text: The ability to command obedience is called
explanation: Authority is the recognised, rightful power to give commands and be obeyed. It differs from mere influence because it carries legitimacy, so people accept a duty to comply.

subject: Government
text: Legitimacy is determined mainly by
explanation: Legitimacy is the popular acceptance of a government or ruler as rightful and entitled to be obeyed. It rests fundamentally on the consent and acceptance of the people.

subject: Government
text: A.V. Dicey popularised the principle of
explanation: The British jurist A.V. Dicey gave the classic modern exposition of the rule of law, stressing the supremacy of law, equality before the law, and rights grounded in ordinary court decisions. He is therefore associated with popularising this principle.

subject: Government
text: The concept of collective responsibility is synonymous with the
explanation: Collective responsibility means the cabinet as a whole is jointly answerable to the legislature and must publicly support government decisions or resign. It is a defining feature of the parliamentary (cabinet) system.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following was the first political party in Nigeria?

options.C: National Council of Nigeria and the Cameroons (NCNC)

explanation: The Nigerian National Democratic Party (NNDP), founded by Herbert Macaulay in 1923, was the first modern political party in Nigeria. It contested the elective seats introduced by the Clifford Constitution.

subject: Government
text: The primary function of government in a state is to
explanation: The most basic function of any government is to preserve order, protect life and property, and secure the state. The maintenance of law and order is therefore its primary function, on which other services depend.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following is a legal source of political authority?
explanation: Legitimate political authority in a democracy flows from the consent of the people expressed through elections. Power derived from the electorate is therefore a legal source of authority, unlike power seized by force.

subject: Government
text: Which organ of government is vested with the responsibility of initiating bills and recommending them to the legislature for consideration?
explanation: Although the legislature enacts laws, many bills, especially money and policy bills, originate from the executive, which drafts and forwards them to the legislature. The executive therefore initiates and recommends such bills for consideration.

subject: Government
text: A representative government can be established through
explanation: A representative government is one in which the people choose those who govern on their behalf. Such a government is established through a free and fair general election, not by force or fraud.

subject: Government
text: Nationalism in Africa eventually led to

options.A: a rapid political awareness among the colonialists
options.B: the alignment of the new states

explanation: African nationalism was the organised demand for self-rule and freedom from colonial domination. Its ultimate result was decolonisation, the withdrawal of the colonial powers and the emergence of independent African states.

subject: Government
text: The first Head of State and Head of Government in Nigeria was
explanation: Under the parliamentary First Republic, the offices of Head of State and Head of Government were separate. They were first combined in one person when General J.T.U. Aguiyi-Ironsi took over after the January 1966 coup.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following is the lowest in the hierarchy of the feudal system?
explanation: Feudal society was a pyramid with the monarch and lords at the top and the serfs at the bottom. The serfs, who worked the land and were tied to it, occupied the lowest rung of the feudal order.

subject: Government
text: Which of the following cannot be found in a constitution?
explanation: A constitution typically contains the organs of government, the rights and duties of citizens, and the distribution of powers. Party manifestoes are documents of individual parties, not part of the constitution.

subject: Government
text: Nigeria's foreign policy of non-alignment was a reaction to
explanation: Non-alignment meant refusing to join either the Western capitalist bloc or the Eastern communist bloc during the Cold War. It was Nigeria's response to the East-West ideological rivalry between the superpowers.

subject: Government
text: Which of these international organisations is the predecessor of the United Nations?
explanation: The League of Nations, established in 1920 after the First World War, was the forerunner of the UN. When it failed to prevent the Second World War it was dissolved and replaced by the United Nations in 1945.

subject: CRS
text: What was upon the face of the deep on the first day of creation?
explanation: Genesis 1:2 states that 'darkness was upon the face of the deep' before God began His creative acts. On the first day, God said 'Let there be light' (v.3), which implies darkness existed before that creative command. The deep was covered with darkness and water, with the Spirit of God hovering over the waters.

subject: CRS
text: 'Let there be lights in the firmament of the heavens to separate the day from the night…' This statement was made by God on the
explanation: According to Genesis 1:14-19, God created the sun, moon, and stars — the 'lights in the firmament' — on the fourth day of creation. The third day involved gathering of waters and creation of vegetation, while the fifth day involved creatures of sea and sky.

subject: CRS
text: The story of the Fall teaches that

options.A: man was made in the image of God
options.B: it is a sin to disobey God
options.C: Adam was less guilty than Eve

explanation: The central moral lesson of the Fall narrative (Genesis 3) is that disobedience to God's commands constitutes sin and brings consequences. Adam and Eve disobeyed God's explicit command not to eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, resulting in their expulsion from Eden and the introduction of suffering and death into the world.

subject: CRS
text: 'On that day all the fountains of the great deep burst forth and the windows of the heavens were opened.' The result of this was a
explanation: Genesis 7:11-12 describes the onset of the great flood: the fountains of the deep burst open and the windows of heaven were opened, resulting in heavy rain that fell for forty days and forty nights. This dual source — subterranean waters and heavenly rain — caused the great deluge that covered the entire earth.

subject: CRS
text: 'Who would have said to Abraham that Sarah would suckle children?...' This was said by
explanation: Genesis 21:7 records Sarah herself making this exclamation of wonder: 'Who would have said to Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age.' Sarah expressed her own amazement and joy at the miraculous birth of Isaac when she was ninety years old.

subject: CRS
text: The quotation, 'Let me not look upon the death of the child' comes from the story about

options.B: the expulsion of Hagar and Ishmael
options.C: Moses when he was placed on the bank of the Nile

explanation: This quotation comes from Genesis 21:16, where Hagar, after being expelled from Abraham's household with her son Ishmael, placed the dying child under a bush and sat at a distance, saying she could not bear to watch him die. God heard the boy's cry and provided water to save them both.

subject: CRS
text: Joseph's dream about his future leadership of his brothers was fulfilled when he became

options.D: the second in command of Pharaoh

explanation: Joseph's dreams (Genesis 37) foretold that his brothers would bow down to him. This was fulfilled when Pharaoh made Joseph second-in-command over all Egypt (Genesis 41:40-43), and his brothers came to Egypt to buy grain during the famine, bowing before him without recognizing him (Genesis 42:6).

subject: CRS
text: The children of Israel cried out to God for help in Egypt because

options.D: Moses failed to look after them

explanation: Exodus 2:23-24 records that the Israelites groaned and cried out because of their bondage — their harsh enslavement and forced labour in Egypt. God heard their groaning and remembered His covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Though male children were also killed, the fundamental cry was against the condition of bondage.

subject: CRS
text: 'What is this we have done, that we have let Israel go from serving us?' The above statement was made by
explanation: Exodus 14:5 records that when Pharaoh was told the Israelites had fled, 'the heart of Pharaoh and his servants was turned against the people, and they said, What is this we have done, that we have let Israel go from serving us?' This regret led Pharaoh to pursue Israel with his army to the Red Sea.

subject: CRS
text: The manna which the Israelites left till the morning in disobedience to God
explanation: Exodus 16:20 states that when some Israelites disobeyed God's instruction and kept manna overnight, 'it bred worms and became foul.' God had commanded them to gather only what they needed for each day, trusting in His daily provision, and hoarding it was an act of disobedience that resulted in this spoilage.

subject: CRS
text: God gave the ten commandments to the people of Israel because He

options.B: wanted their days to be long
options.C: redeemed them from slavery in Egypt
options.D: frowned at those who took His Name in vain

explanation: Exodus 20:2 opens the Ten Commandments with God's declaration: 'I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.' This redemptive act is the basis and motivation for the commandments — God gave them as a response to His prior act of deliverance, calling Israel to live as a redeemed people.

subject: CRS
text: '…Choose this day whom you will serve,…as for me and my house, we will serve the LORD.' The Israelites' response to the above statement was '…we

options.A: also will serve the LORD, for He is our God'
options.B: cannot serve the LORD, for He is a holy God'
options.C: will serve the LORD though He is a jealous God'
options.D: cannot put away the foreign gods among us because our fathers served them'

explanation: Joshua 24:18 records the people's positive response to Joshua's challenge at Shechem: 'We also will serve the LORD, for He is our God.' The people recounted God's mighty acts — bringing their ancestors out of Egypt, driving out the Amorites — as reasons for their commitment to serve the LORD.

subject: CRS
text: God's anger was kindled against Eli and his children because Eli did not

options.A: train his children in the way of the LORD
options.C: apply appropriate sanctions on his children when due
options.D: know the gravity of their actions

explanation: 1 Samuel 3:13 records God's judgment: 'For I have told him that I will judge his house for ever for the iniquity which he knew; because his sons made themselves vile, and he restrained them not.' Eli knew of his sons' wickedness and warned them verbally (1 Sam 2:23-25), but he failed to apply proper disciplinary action — he did not sanction or remove them from priestly office.

subject: CRS
text: The prophets whom Saul met at Gibeath-elohim as a confirmation that the LORD had anointed him to rule over God's people were
explanation: 1 Samuel 10:5-6 describes a band of prophets coming down from the high place with instruments — harp, tambourine, flute, and lyre — prophesying in an ecstatic manner. These were ecstatic prophets, characterized by their frenzied, Spirit-induced behaviour. Saul joined them and 'prophesied among them,' giving rise to the saying 'Is Saul also among the prophets?'

subject: CRS
text: While David was being anointed king, he received
explanation: 1 Samuel 16:13 records that when Samuel anointed David in the midst of his brothers, 'the Spirit of the LORD came mightily upon David from that day forward.' The anointing with oil was the outward sign, but the accompanying gift of God's Spirit was the real empowerment for David's kingship.

subject: CRS
text: Against whom were the Israelites fighting when King David committed adultery with Uriah's wife?
explanation: 2 Samuel 11:1 states that 'at the time when kings go out to battle,' David sent Joab and his army to fight against the Ammonites and besiege Rabbah. It was during this military campaign that David remained in Jerusalem, saw Bathsheba bathing, and committed adultery with her. Uriah, her husband, was a soldier in this Ammonite campaign.

subject: CRS
text: According to Psalm 51, the sacrifice acceptable to God is
explanation: Psalm 51:17 declares: 'The sacrifice acceptable to God is a broken spirit; a broken and contrite heart, O God, you will not despise.' David wrote this psalm after his sin with Bathsheba, recognizing that outward sacrificial rituals (burnt offerings) could not substitute for genuine inner repentance — a truly broken and humble spirit before God.

subject: CRS
text: Solomon could devote his attention to the building of the house of the LORD because

options.A: of the supplies from the Queen of Sheba
options.C: there was neither adversary nor misfortune
options.D: of David's purchase of the site for the building

explanation: 1 Kings 5:4 records Solomon's message to Hiram: 'But now the LORD my God has given me rest on every side; there is neither adversary nor misfortune.' This peace on all sides — unlike David's reign, which was marked by wars — gave Solomon the political stability needed to undertake the massive temple construction project.

subject: CRS
text: The immediate cause of the division of the kingdom was
explanation: 1 Kings 12:13-16 shows that when the people asked Rehoboam to lighten their burdens, he foolishly rejected the counsel of the elders and instead threatened to make their yoke heavier. This harsh and unwise response was the immediate trigger that caused the ten northern tribes to rebel and form the separate kingdom of Israel under Jeroboam.

subject: CRS
text: 'Arise, go to Zarephath, which belongs to Sidon, and dwell there. Behold, I have commanded a widow there to feed you.' What exactly necessitated this event?

options.A: The drought caused by Elijah's curse
options.B: Ahab's evil in the sight of the LORD
options.C: The ravens ceased to feed Elijah

explanation: 1 Kings 17:7-9 explains that 'after a while the brook dried up, because there was no rain in the land.' Since the brook Cherith dried up, the ravens could no longer sustain Elijah there. God then directed him to Zarephath to be fed by a widow. The immediate cause of his relocation was that the ravens' ability to feed him was cut off by the drought drying up the brook.

subject: CRS
text: 'Blessed be the Lord God of Israel,…' is a hymn of praise by

options.A: the Virgin Mary at the Annunciation
options.B: Zechariah at the birth of John the Baptist
options.C: Simeon at the presentation of Jesus in the Temple
options.D: Elizabeth at the visit of Mary

explanation: Luke 1:68-79 records the Benedictus — the hymn beginning 'Blessed be the Lord God of Israel' — spoken by Zechariah when his son John the Baptist was born and he regained his speech. He praised God for fulfilling the promise of a Messiah and for the special role his son John would play as the forerunner of the Lord.

subject: CRS
text: According to the birth and infancy narratives in St. Matthew, the angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream three times. In the second appearance, he instructed Joseph to

options.A: accept that Mary was a virgin

explanation: In Matthew's infancy narrative, the angel appeared to Joseph three times: first to accept Mary's pregnancy (Matt 1:20), second to flee to Egypt (Matt 2:13), and third to return from Egypt to Israel (Matt 2:19-20). The second appearance therefore instructed Joseph to return from exile in Egypt, as Herod who sought the child's life had died.

subject: CRS
text: 'Lord, now lettest thou thy servant depart in peace, according to thy word; for mine eyes have seen thy salvation…' This was said at the dedication of Jesus by
explanation: Luke 2:29-32 records the Nunc Dimittis spoken by Simeon, a righteous and devout man in Jerusalem to whom the Holy Spirit had revealed he would not die before seeing the Lord's Christ. When Mary and Joseph brought Jesus to the temple for dedication, Simeon took the child in his arms and uttered this canticle of praise and peaceful departure.

subject: CRS
text: 'You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?' This was addressed to
explanation: Matthew 3:7 records that when John the Baptist saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to his baptism, he said to them: 'You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?' John challenged these religious leaders to produce genuine fruits of repentance rather than relying on their ancestral connection to Abraham.

subject: CRS
text: The second temptation of Jesus according to Luke's account of the gospel was

options.A: giving the kingdoms of the world to Jesus if he would worship the devil
options.B: a command for a stone to become bread
options.C: jumping down from the pinnacle of the temple
options.D: denying the name of God, the Father

explanation: Luke's account of the temptations (Luke 4:1-13) presents them in this order: first, turn stones to bread; second, the devil shows Jesus all the kingdoms of the world and offers them if Jesus would worship him; third, jump from the pinnacle of the temple. This order differs from Matthew's account. Therefore in Luke, the second temptation was the offer of worldly kingdoms in exchange for worship.

subject: CRS
text: What did Christ say should be the qualification of whoever was to be first or great among the apostles?

options.C: Giving one's life as a ransom
options.D: Drinking from the same cup as Jesus

explanation: Mark 10:44 records Jesus's teaching: 'Whoever would be first among you must be slave of all.' When James and John requested positions of honour, Jesus used this as a teaching moment, contrasting the world's concept of greatness (lording over others) with Kingdom greatness (servant-leadership and self-giving service to all).

subject: CRS
text: A would-be disciple, who puts his hand to the plough and looks back, lacks
explanation: Luke 9:62 records Jesus saying: 'No one who puts his hand to the plough and looks back is fit for the kingdom of God.' A ploughman who looks back will make crooked furrows — the metaphor illustrates that true discipleship requires undivided, forward-looking commitment. Looking back represents divided loyalty and lack of total dedication to following Jesus.

subject: CRS
text: In which miracle story was Jesus mistaken for a ghost by his disciples?

options.A: The feeding of the five thousand
options.B: Calming of the storm and sea

explanation: Matthew 14:26 (and Mark 6:49) records that when the disciples saw Jesus walking on the sea toward their boat, they were terrified and cried out 'It is a ghost!' They mistook Jesus for a phantom because it was contrary to natural expectation. Jesus then called out to them: 'Take heart, it is I; have no fear.'

subject: CRS
text: According to Luke, the healing of the paralytic was intended to show that

options.B: Jesus has authority to forgive sins
options.C: the Holy Spirit makes intercession for sinners

explanation: Luke 5:24 records Jesus saying: 'But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins…' and then healing the paralytic. Jesus deliberately connected the physical healing to the forgiveness of sins to demonstrate that He possesses divine authority to forgive — which the scribes and Pharisees considered blasphemy, since only God can forgive sins.

subject: CRS
text: The story of the healing of blind Bartimaeus teaches that

options.A: prayer is very basic to healing
options.B: there is need to persistently shout for attention
options.C: Jesus is the Son of David
options.D: faith is a pre-requisite to healing

explanation: Mark 10:52 records Jesus telling Bartimaeus: 'Go your way; your faith has made you well.' Bartimaeus demonstrated persistent faith by calling out despite being rebuked by the crowd, and Jesus attributed the healing directly to his faith. The story illustrates that faith — trust in Jesus's power and willingness to heal — is the essential prerequisite for receiving healing.

subject: CRS
text: The parable of the prodigal son emphasizes that

options.A: it is not good for the son and the father to quarrel
options.C: the father loves the second son more than the first

explanation: The parable of the Prodigal Son (Luke 15:11-32) centres on the younger son's repentance ('I will arise and go to my father') and the father's joyful reconciliation and restoration upon the son's return. The father's running to meet him, embracing him, and celebrating illustrate God's eager forgiveness of those who genuinely repent and return to Him.

subject: CRS
text: According to Matthew's account of the gospel, the most important divine requirement for entering the kingdom of heaven is
explanation: Matthew 7:21 records Jesus saying: 'Not everyone who says to me, Lord, Lord, shall enter the kingdom of heaven, but he who does the will of my Father who is in heaven.' Jesus emphasized that outward religious activities — even miracles and prophecy done in His name — are insufficient without genuine obedience to God's will.

subject: CRS
text: After their mission, the seventy disciples reported to Jesus that

options.B: they enjoyed the hospitality of people
options.D: demons were subject to them in His name

explanation: Luke 10:17 records: 'The seventy returned with joy, saying, Lord, even the demons are subject to us in your name!' This was their most remarkable experience — that through the authority of Jesus's name, they had power over demonic forces. Jesus responded by saying He saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven.

subject: CRS
text: The appearance of Moses and Elijah at the transfiguration underscores the importance in the ministry of Jesus of
explanation: Moses represents the Law (Torah) and Elijah represents the Prophets — the two major divisions of the Hebrew Scriptures. Their appearance with Jesus at the transfiguration (Luke 9:30-31, Matt 17:3) signifies that Jesus is the fulfillment of both the Law and the Prophets, and that His ministry stands in continuity with and brings to completion all that God had previously revealed.

subject: CRS
text: 'This is my beloved Son; listen to him.' This was said on the occasion of Jesus'
explanation: Mark 9:7 (and parallels Matthew 17:5, Luke 9:35) records that a cloud overshadowed the disciples at the transfiguration and a voice came from the cloud saying 'This is my beloved Son; listen to him.' While similar words were spoken at the baptism, the specific command 'listen to him' identifies this as the transfiguration account.

subject: CRS
text: '…My house shall be a house of prayer; but you have made it a den of robbers.' Just before making this statement, the speaker

options.C: drove out those who sold in the temple

explanation: Luke 19:45-46 records that Jesus entered the temple and began to drive out those who sold there, saying: 'My house shall be a house of prayer, but you have made it a den of robbers.' The cleansing action preceded the declaration — Jesus first physically expelled the merchants, then gave the scriptural rationale for His action (quoting Isaiah 56:7 and Jeremiah 7:11).

subject: CRS
text: Which of the following questions did the high priest ask Jesus during His trial?

options.A: "Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?"
options.C: "Are you the King of the Jews?"

explanation: Mark 14:61 records that during Jesus's trial before the Sanhedrin, the high priest asked: 'Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?' Jesus answered affirmatively, and the high priest tore his robes, declaring it blasphemy. The phrase 'the Blessed' is a Jewish reverential way of referring to God, making this question a direct inquiry about Jesus's divine identity.

subject: CRS
text: '…Do not weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children…' These words were addressed by Jesus to the
explanation: Luke 23:28 records Jesus turning to a great multitude following Him on the way to the cross and saying: 'Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.' This prophetic warning pointed to the coming destruction of Jerusalem (AD 70) and the suffering that its inhabitants would endure.

subject: CRS
text: What was the first statement of Jesus on the cross according to Luke's account of the gospel?

options.B: "Today you shall be with me in paradise."
options.C: "Father forgive them for they do not know what they do."

explanation: Luke 23:34 records the first word from the cross in Luke's account: 'Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do.' This intercessory prayer, offered as Jesus was being nailed to the cross, demonstrates His extraordinary love and mercy even toward His executioners — fulfilling His own teaching to pray for enemies.

subject: CRS
text: Very early in the morning, on the first day of the week, three women went to the tomb of Jesus to

options.D: see whether the soldiers had carried the body away

explanation: Mark 16:1-2 records that Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices 'so that they might go and anoint him.' They came very early on the first day of the week to complete the burial anointing that had been interrupted by the onset of the Sabbath. Their purpose was to reverently anoint Jesus's body.

subject: CRS
text: '…Let all the house of Israel therefore know assuredly that God has made him both Lord and Christ, this Jesus whom you crucified…' As a reaction to this declaration, the Jews

options.A: decided to kill Peter and the apostles
options.B: went back to their houses in anger
options.C: were deeply troubled and asked what they should do

explanation: Acts 2:37 records that when the people heard Peter's Pentecost sermon concluding with this declaration about Jesus, 'they were cut to the heart, and said to Peter and the rest of the apostles, Brethren, what shall we do?' This deep conviction led to Peter's call to repent and be baptized, resulting in about three thousand people being added to the church that day.

subject: CRS
text: '…You have not lied to men but to God.' The immediate effect of this statement of Peter on Ananias was that he
explanation: Acts 5:5 records: 'When Ananias heard these words, he fell down and died.' Peter exposed that Ananias had lied not merely to the apostles but to God Himself when he secretly kept part of the money while pretending to give all. The immediate divine judgment was Ananias's sudden death, striking fear into all who heard of it.

subject: CRS
text: The apostles created a new office in the ministry besides prayer and preaching, in order to address the problems of
explanation: Acts 6:1-6 records that the Hellenist widows were being neglected in the daily distribution of food while the Hebrew widows were not — an unjust situation. To address this injustice, the twelve proposed choosing seven men full of the Spirit and wisdom to oversee the daily distribution, creating the office of deacon so that the apostles could focus on prayer and preaching.

subject: CRS
text: Saul's encounter with the Risen Christ on the road to Damascus convinced him that

options.B: Jesus is the Son of God
options.C: the faith of his father was right
options.D: he should have gone to Damascus with more armed escort

explanation: Acts 9:20 records that immediately after his conversion and recovery, Saul 'in the synagogues immediately he proclaimed Jesus, saying He is the Son of God.' The Damascus road encounter — seeing and hearing the risen Christ — convinced Paul beyond doubt that Jesus was indeed the divine Son of God, completely reversing his prior opposition.

subject: CRS
text: When Saul escaped from Damascus to Jerusalem after his conversion, the disciples in Jerusalem

options.D: were full of sympathy for him

explanation: Acts 9:26 records: 'And when he had come to Jerusalem he attempted to join the disciples; and they were all afraid of him, for they did not believe that he was a disciple.' The Jerusalem church feared Saul because of his notorious reputation as a persecutor. It was only through Barnabas's intervention that they accepted his conversion as genuine.

subject: CRS
text: Paul and Barnabas were referred to as 'gods' in Lystra because they

options.A: were agents of the unknown God

explanation: Acts 14:8-12 records that in Lystra, Paul healed a man who had been crippled from birth. When the crowds saw what Paul had done, they shouted 'The gods have come down to us in the likeness of men!' calling Barnabas Zeus and Paul Hermes. The miraculous healing of the crippled man was the direct cause of this reaction.

subject: CRS
text: '…Abstain from what has been sacrificed to idols and from blood and from what is strangled and from unchastity…' To which issue was the above a response?
explanation: Acts 15 records the Jerusalem Council, which was convened specifically to address the circumcision controversy — whether Gentile believers needed to be circumcised and keep the Mosaic law for salvation. The letter issued by the council (Acts 15:29) contained these abstentions as the decree, not as a salvation requirement but as practical guidelines for Jewish-Gentile fellowship.

subject: CRS
text: '…I am a Pharisee, a son of Pharisees; with respect to the hope and resurrection of the dead I am on trial.' This was part of the address of Paul to
explanation: Acts 23:1-6 records Paul's defence before the Sanhedrin (the Jewish council), where he declared he was a Pharisee and that the issue was the resurrection of the dead. This cleverly divided the Sanhedrin between Pharisees (who believed in resurrection) and Sadducees (who did not), causing a dispute that led to Paul's removal for his own safety.

subject: CRS
text: According to Paul, which of the following is the greatest?
explanation: 1 Corinthians 13:13 states: 'So faith, hope, love abide, these three; but the greatest of these is love.' Paul's great hymn to love (agape) in 1 Corinthians 13 concludes by affirming that love surpasses all spiritual gifts, all knowledge, even faith that moves mountains — because love is the very nature of God and never fails.

subject: CRS
text: According to the creation account of Genesis 1, God charged man to

options.C: have dominion over all the earth

explanation: Genesis 1:28 records the cultural mandate: 'And God blessed them, and God said to them, Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth and subdue it; and have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the birds of the air and over every living thing that moves upon the earth.' This charge to exercise stewardship and dominion over creation was a key part of God's purpose for humanity.

subject: CRS
text: When Elijah escaped to the brook Cherith, he was fed by
explanation: According to 1 Kings 17:4-6, God commanded ravens to bring Elijah bread and meat at the brook Cherith. The ravens obeyed, feeding Elijah morning and evening during the drought. This miracle demonstrated God's providential care for His prophet even in extreme circumstances.

subject: CRS
text: The contest on Mount Carmel was to prove that

options.A: Baal was the god of Israel
options.C: God was supreme to other gods

explanation: In 1 Kings 18, Elijah challenged the 450 prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel to determine which deity was the true God. When fire fell from heaven and consumed Elijah's offering but Baal did not respond to his prophets, it proved conclusively that the LORD, not Baal, was supreme over all gods. The people responded, 'The LORD, He is God!'

subject: CRS
text: Naboth did not agree to release his vineyard to Ahab because

options.A: the amount offered for it was too small
options.B: it was his major source of income
options.D: it was the inheritance of his fathers

explanation: In 1 Kings 21:3, Naboth replied to King Ahab, 'The LORD forbid that I should give you the inheritance of my fathers.' Under Mosaic law (Leviticus 25), ancestral land was to remain within the tribe and family as a divine trust, making its sale contrary to God's commandments. Naboth's refusal was a matter of religious obedience, not economics.

subject: CRS
text: What did Gehazi deceitfully receive from Naaman?

options.A: two talents of silver and two festal garments
options.B: two talents of silver and two shekels of gold
options.C: two festal garments and two talents of gold
options.D: two thousand shekels and two festal garments

explanation: In 2 Kings 5:22-23, Gehazi ran after Naaman and falsely claimed that Elisha wanted two talents of silver and two festal garments for two young prophets. Naaman willingly gave him exactly these items. As punishment for his greed and deceit, Gehazi and his descendants were struck with Naaman's leprosy.

subject: CRS
text: The greatest achievement of King Josiah of Judah was his

options.B: sweeping reforms of the religious life of his people
options.D: training a worthy successor before his death

explanation: 2 Kings 22–23 records that after the Book of the Law was found in the Temple, Josiah initiated comprehensive religious reforms: he destroyed idols, removed pagan priests, broke down high places, and reinstated the Passover. His reforms were unparalleled — 2 Kings 23:25 states there was no king before or after him who turned to the LORD with all his heart as Josiah did.

subject: CRS
text: Jerusalem fell in 586 B.C. to the Babylonian King called
explanation: According to 2 Kings 25 and 2 Chronicles 36, Nebuchadnezzar II of Babylon captured Jerusalem in 586 BC, destroyed Solomon's Temple, and carried the remaining Judeans into exile in Babylon. Nabopolassar was Nebuchadnezzar's father, while Asshurbanapal was an Assyrian king. This event marked the end of the Kingdom of Judah.

subject: CRS
text: And the King (Artaxerxes) said to me, 'Why is your face sad, seeing you are not sick? This is nothing else but sadness of the heart.' Why was the addressee sad?

options.A: The condition of Jerusalem was deplorable
options.B: He was fasting because of Jerusalem
options.C: Enemies of Jerusalem were laughing at him
options.D: He was in exile in Babylon

explanation: In Nehemiah 2:2-3, Nehemiah explained to King Artaxerxes that he was sad because 'the city, the place of my fathers' graves, lies waste, and its gates have been destroyed by fire.' Nehemiah had received a distressing report about Jerusalem's broken-down walls and burnt gates (Nehemiah 1:3), and his grief was over the deplorable state of the holy city.

subject: CRS
text: Although Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego were cast into the fiery furnace, King Nebuchadnezzar was astonished to see four men in the furnace. He said the fourth man was like
explanation: In Daniel 3:25, Nebuchadnezzar looked into the furnace and exclaimed, 'I see four men unbound, walking in the middle of the fire, and they are not hurt; and the appearance of the fourth is like a son of the gods.' This was Nebuchadnezzar's own pagan description of a divine being. Christians traditionally interpret this fourth figure as a pre-incarnate appearance of Christ.

subject: CRS
text: In the belly of the fish Jonah prayed to God to
explanation: Jonah 2:1-9 records Jonah's prayer from inside the great fish, which is a prayer of thanksgiving and supplication for deliverance. Jonah cried out, 'I called out to the LORD, out of my distress, and he answered me' (Jonah 2:2), and he trusted that God would bring him up from the pit. The prayer is focused on God rescuing him from death, not on forgiving him or punishing Nineveh.

subject: CRS
text: The sins of Nineveh notwithstanding, God spared the inhabitants because

options.A: Jonah was reluctant to deliver the message to them
options.B: the people responded positively to God's mercy
options.C: Nineveh was made up of a large population which did not know its right from its left
options.D: Nineveh was a great and prosperous city with one hundred and twenty thousand inhabitants

explanation: Jonah 3:10 states, 'When God saw what they did, how they turned from their evil way, God relented of the disaster that he had said he would do to them.' The Ninevites believed God's message, proclaimed a fast, and repented in sackcloth from the greatest to the least. God's response was directly tied to their genuine repentance and positive response to His mercy.

subject: CRS
text: Daniel was distinguished above all other satraps and presidents because he

options.B: had an excellent spirit in him
options.C: refused to worship the golden image
